## **Texas Faces Freezing Temperatures**
A strong cold front is [bringing freezing temperatures to Texas](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGurCAQBuCnwU4DI7haUNzGbm97SzPCsJIgGCDr7n36E32F000yk_nz_f-eyxN98PH1F3fS9ePSEuksgWqlvJgUrMsp1kKBIhaHdaNsPS3AQbUCWiEXiz58F9lYvXI7iYa0eAzqIfphnJpNr7JfB0TgY28UOFJozSqVs2hUz41pvL6eCRBSABcwdGqarlkB58jlODDJyfqaclc3qvTB4sI3vSl3Ju1N0FutR2H9HwYzg_k8z27_FowYa4o-XUcM5kt1bekmMJgxV28CMZgvZXszW5eJ_lN7h7QgoB8n3nfHdjRZ2-P0IfgyPJjkrx19uPPr71p7a_gJAAD__6cngdo) starting Saturday as part of a polar vortex sweeping the U.S. The Texas Panhandle will see temperatures in the teens first, with wind chills 5 to 10 degrees colder than actual temperatures. Forecasters urge residents to [limit time outdoors and bring pets inside](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGv4yAMB_BPQzYicAiQgeGWbL31xogGU5BoiIBr2vv0p-Qtb3-bpb9t-ec_t-VmY4rb47d9omlvn5cNj5qwNSzLmpPzJW-tYsLNVm9bwOIiLsBgpBwoF4uzMX0W0TlzZ27iHRqu5Kj4IPXUBSMmfVeD1nbUUg93peXonRoUg0kq7aGL5lzGgQsOjIPsx2k66xEYs0xoSQRDF1sufQvY8G2rT5_8wtKv-dklE1rbKxl-EZgJzMdx9GsoeTtTAvPJITCH_Le2vNFrnsBsS4trwm_JiaWX9quJHnhxqS-I_5ACh0FoDf0e9q4Ytx8xpVilIoI9njam65z2sye-DPwPAAD__5xdhdg), though no snow or wintry mix is expected--just sunny skies and arctic air. The cold front arrived late Saturday with temperatures potentially dipping below freezing across much of the state.
## Selena's Father Dies
Abraham Quintanilla Jr., father and [former manager of Tejano music legend Selena](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2O8yAQQE-DOywY47-C4mvc5Wu3tCZmHJAweIHEye1XTvYG2400T0_z5usyX9B5F27_cSNdnmucAx3ZUymU5iV6s6YYSiZPAfOKxVIyjmYQ0HIJXKrZoPOvWVVGX4UZZUVa9l3by6Ybxsrqbljl0GArVljGVo39ihKlbCQq05jFVE6fMglSSRASurodx3NuQQgUauiYEmRciakulgo9Ma_-FR-U6iVulde2lD2z5h-DicF0HEdt4z2XGBabYnCLpxNkMJ1lDKbfLX-rGEwlUTAu3BhMmMrJM5g-xfxTzPGa0OLGv-8uFAzOe-TGUeYgQYluaOvd7lXSZj-c9y53PVPitqHz7xvL3z770PATAAD__37mjsw), died Saturday at age 86, his son A.B. Quintanilla III announced on Instagram. The Corpus Christi musician formed Selena y Los Dinos in the 1980s and guided his daughter's rise from local performances to international stardom before her murder in 1995. Following Selena's death, Abraham [devoted himself to preserving](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2upDAQhE9jMo_sxmAIHGxC9jbdEPXg5tErY8_inr_br2bmCC8rVUml-urP1_yFnDh__8adgjzWMme610QidMxLSXE9SpZKiTLWFWWjIzLNYKDTFrR1c0ROz9k1MZxNHG1Dwfq-87bth7HZQtfiaA0NBB4AW2Os9-NovKcVwZFvOLzKLFhnwVjoT904vnQHxqBxQ6-cochSjpNsJPTAuqZnudFxWsrepLCJXKpqfymYFEx4XvwrUDBdSpW3c-CGu_535SyYOSXUkVA2_eHRQn8xF71fKy-al5L1h1dvWHVkirqWrPGsmVlXfFYFkx1gaJ0ZFEzNEeLlzilx7b1y5ntHTu9t8rNHbwH-BwAA__8wRolo) her legacy, working on projects including the 1997 biographical film starring Edward James Olmos and Netflix's recent _Selena: The Series_. No cause of death has been released.
## Emma Stone Sells Austin Estate
Oscar-winning actress Emma Stone and husband Dave McCary sold their Austin estate after spending three years renovating the property but never moving in. [See photos here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2KwzAQhU8jdwrSxJbjQsU27rLtlmZsjSPB2ArSkJ_bLw7sCbYZBt7j--D9XKcrJk777Rs38vJa87TTszKJUJmWzGEteZdKTDvWFSVSCYkmMNBpC9q2U8DE76ltgp9NGGxD3vau6-3ZXYYmery4BYwd5r7H2biwzGi6YSGzOnLQY5P8AbNgWwvGgjt1w3D8HRiDpr041RoKSXI5SSShF9aV3_lB5bTkrWEfRe5Vnb8UjArG_X3PVY5IwXiQFYwW_k4hZE1VUEjBSNuGukreSUesumYOOlLRH4mOeTtKTfHh_kzMqbpetea2YeKPWv432MPDbwAAAP__Jad9fQ). The couple [purchased the 1940s home in 2021](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO3SAQRb8Gd1gwtvGjcBEpcrdpU1pjGNZowaxg8vze30fOL6Qbae490rm_P7YPjCmen78w08KvULaTrpaImermSvKhlpMbJTqxBeSDqo-0gYJJapB63DzG9N7Gzi-78lZ3tOjZTLMezMN2x4JBWwrTjtMIO-2DHoIzeiYPypmwmy4uN0yDHjUoDaafrL3vCZRCNT6MGBX5yKX2fBDTC1tI7_Kk2ruSu7QczN9NDD8ErALW67r6SpjuvCtZwHoLCVgdJdpr5Le835IaI5OAlXJG2bicJLF9NQlGTjLHlGI5ZShVhkrtSHftLE9k8hL_NI6nPEq-AWJYcyvuK3ox_FTazqO1GqzezWC1BnRTgBkeDgwo6uriv6-b3swsRvWZMaZ_Ivx_8z8X-BsAAP__E5CYqQ) with plans to relocate from California to Texas full-time, then listed it for $26.5 million in May. The 1.24-acre Tarrytown property includes a five-bedroom main house, a two-bedroom guest house, and a garage with a screening room. Listing agent Eric Moreland told _The Wall Street Journal_ the couple's business interests expanded in New York, making it [impractical to move to Texas](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2u6yAQBOCnsTsiwBibguI26XLbU1rrsASkNVhm8_f2R8krnG6kkb6R5ueyXCBTLrf_sKHnV6xLwWcjZMZjuVYK8aiFGxIWaBE44REyLlrqUSgtlFkCZHovpg9-lcGpHr2a7Dipwc6uT35d0c1yldagjoMaJZjoxqgni1ccVuiz_2BKK6O0VNqeRuc-edRSgjSz7YzEkLkeJ07I-IIW6V0feJyudevJJ-a9dcO_Tp87fd6x7oSfqtNn3DYQjWtB0ZCoCbg3zkV8FZHqhuKZOYl0byuUIPZUuTah1GznwY394cP-zES52akz8rZBpu8q_-2rh9e_AQAA__9bxn5b) now, though they still hope to live in Austin eventually.

Marvin Leonard, known as "Mr. Marvin," opened his first department store in Fort Worth. His brother Obadiah, known as "Mr. Obie," joined him the next year to form , which thrived for many years on a business model encapsulated in its slogan, "More merchandise for less money." The chain was sold to the Tandy Corporation in 1967 and to Dillard's in 1974. We're reporting today on a new Austin policy that for drivers who used a rideshare or sober ride home. Learn how to have a ticket waived in Around Texas below. When you need gifts that people will actually love, is your shortcut. If you're anything like us at The Flyover , you'll love that today's sponsor, , is offering a luxury 5-cigar sampler that delivers premium flavor without the premium markup. Abilene ☁️ 44/30° | Amarillo ☀️ 46/29° | Austin ☁️ 53/43° | Brownsville 🌦️ 66/56° | Corpus Christi ☁️ 62/51° | DFW ☀️ 46/32° | El Paso ☀️ 61/45° | Houston ☁️ 56/42° | Laredo ☁️ 62/53° | Lubbock ☁️ 48/33° | Midland ☁️ 41/31° | San Antonio ☁️ 55/46° | Tyler ☀️ 47/35° | Waco 🌤️ 50/38° Take The Flyover with you. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog--your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Texas Faces Freezing Temperatures A strong cold front is starting Saturday as part of a polar vortex sweeping the U.S. The Texas Panhandle will see temperatures in the teens first, with wind chills 5 to 10 degrees colder than actual temperatures. Forecasters urge residents to , though no snow or wintry mix is expected--just sunny skies and arctic air. The cold front arrived late Saturday with temperatures potentially dipping below freezing across much of the state. Selena's Father Dies Abraham Quintanilla Jr., father and , died Saturday at age 86, his son A.B. Quintanilla III announced on Instagram. The Corpus Christi musician formed Selena y Los Dinos in the 1980s and guided his daughter's rise from local performances to international stardom before her murder in 1995. Following Selena's death, Abraham her legacy, working on projects including the 1997 biographical film starring Edward James Olmos and Netflix's recent Selena: The Series. No cause of death has been released. Emma Stone Sells Austin Estate Oscar-winning actress Emma Stone and husband Dave McCary sold their Austin estate after spending three years renovating the property but never moving in. . The couple with plans to relocate from California to Texas full-time, then listed it for $26.5 million in May. The 1.24-acre Tarrytown property includes a five-bedroom main house, a two-bedroom guest house, and a garage with a screening room. Listing agent Eric Moreland told The Wall Street Journal the couple's business interests expanded in New York, making it now, though they still hope to live in Austin eventually. | Flying together with our sponsor We'll be honest with you--BestCigarPrices.com isn't just another client to us. name: Don Jr.
subject: A special thank you for you,Patriot
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
My father and I couldn’t do this without patriots like you. Open this note of gratitude. |
Fellow American, it's Don Jr. I wanted to take a moment to **express my heartfelt gratitude** for **your unwavering support**. My father and I know that this fight **isn't possible without patriots like you** who are willing to stand up for what's right.

We're honored to have you on our team, **Fellow American.**

**[As a small token of our appreciation, I'd like to share a special message with you. | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: An everyday man taps his credit card at the gas pump. He just tapped his credit card at the gas station terminal... | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | An everyday man taps his credit card at the gas pump. Hey everybody. This is my weekend post where I look back at the news of the week, pull out a few stories I think matter, and give you my quick summaries and opinions. My goal is not to tell you what to think, but to offer a different angle you might not have considered.
Before we get into it, I want to say thank you. I received more responses to my last newsletter on crime than almost anything I have written. Over a dozen direct emails and several thoughtful comments. About ninety percent were substantive and respectful, and I appreciate that.
The most common criticism was that I did not focus on guns and gun violence. That omission was intentional. Not because guns do not matter, but because the conversation often stops there. Once guns are mentioned, everything else gets crowded out. Wealth disparities, education, transportation, housing stability, food security, mental health, and community investment all disappear. I wanted to have a broader conversation.
A dedicated post on guns and the Second Amendment will come later.
Now, onto the stories.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5b40bd0d-564c-4814-9ed4-d3e342b07d94?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
**Netflix, Paramount, Warner Bros., and the Consolidation of Media Power**
The biggest story that caught my attention this week was the reported bidding activity around Warner Bros. Discovery and its media assets.
Reports indicate that Netflix explored a potential acquisition or major transaction involving Warner Bros. Discovery's studio and streaming businesses, including HBO, with valuations discussed in the range of roughly $83 billion. While no finalized agreement has been announced, the reporting signaled serious interest and highlighted how valuable legacy media assets still appear on paper.
Shortly after those reports, Paramount Global was linked to a hostile counter-bid in the range of $108 billion. What makes the Paramount side of this particularly interesting is not just the price, but the political and financial proximity of the people involved. Larry Ellison, a major Trump donor, has been widely reported as a key figure connected to Paramount's strategic direction. In parallel, Jared Kushner's private equity firm, Affinity Partners, has been reported as participating in or facilitating financing tied to Middle Eastern sovereign wealth capital. These are not neutral or apolitical actors. These are individuals and entities deeply embedded in global finance and political power.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d612edf7-33f5-433b-856d-91af1d1b5bd2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Larry Ellison
I have said this consistently and I will continue to say it. If you want to understand where money and influence are moving in this era, you look at Trump adjacent individuals and institutions. Proximity to power matters. Political access matters. Regulatory discretion matters. And there is a clear pattern of capital flowing through networks that sit close to the executive branch and its allies. That does not require conspiracy.
At the same time, I am not convinced this strategy guarantees cultural relevance or influence.
Legacy media is losing its grip on younger generations. Kids and teenagers are not waiting for HBO premieres. They are consuming short form vertical video, livestreams, creator driven platforms, and algorithm curated content. Even adults increasingly multitask through movies and scripted television with a phone in their hand. Attention has fractured. The market has shifted.
Owning legacy intellectual property does not automatically translate into influence over the next generation. Nostalgia still has value, especially for people my age, but nostalgia alone does not build cultural dominance going forward.
This deal, if it moves forward in any form, may consolidate ownership and wealth. Whether it consolidates influence is far less certain. Cultural power today is earned differently than it was twenty years ago, and buying the past does not guarantee control of the future.
* * *
**Cuyahoga County Debt**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/de556da9-0d7c-4fc4-8954-8fb3e335b9e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Another thing that hit my radar this week is Cuyahoga County's debt picture and the sheer number of large projects coming down the pipe.
Cuyahoga County is carrying about $1.5 billion in long term debt. It is still big enough to matter. It is still big enough to constrain services.
The bigger point is not the exact debt number. The point is the trajectory. You have major capital projects stacking up on top of each other, at a time when budgets are already tight and social services are already taking hits.
The new jail campus is priced around $890 million right now, and county officials have openly said final construction pricing is not locked yet. I do not care what anyone tells you. These projects almost never come in under budget. Inflation, procurement, and change orders are real. If you want to be honest, you should assume it ends up higher than the current figure.
Then you have the Browns situation, and all the connected infrastructure conversations. Then you have big institutional funding pressures across the region, including public health systems and hospital networks trying to plug holes.
My core concern is simple. Debt on the books is not automatically bad. Debt can be strategic.
But a growing pile of long term obligations, combined with a horizon full of new obligations, without a clear plan for what gets cut, what gets funded, and how the county stabilizes the finances long term, is a recipe for the same thing we always do. We pretend the bill does not exist until the bill shows up, and then we act shocked that we have to cut services that real people rely on.
* * *
**Ohio Marijuana: What the Legislature Is Actually Doing, and What I Think About It**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/06de331c-8c7e-4af5-823c-8bc22d946b0b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Hemp intoxicating packaging made to look like candy.
When **Ohio** voters passed marijuana legalization, they passed a **statutory resolution** , not a constitutional amendment. That distinction matters, even if people do not like hearing it. A statute can be rewritten, adjusted, or constrained by the legislature. A constitutional amendment cannot be touched without another vote of the people.
I voted against the resolution for that exact reason. Not because I oppose legalization, but because I knew, with full clarity, that vague language would invite legislative intervention. That is not hindsight. That was obvious at the time.
Now that the legislature has acted, people are acting shocked. They should not be.
Here is what is actually in the bill, point by point, and what I think about each provision.
1. **Ban on intoxicating hemp products outside licensed dispensaries**

This provision would restrict intoxicating hemp-derived products, such as delta-8 and similar THC variants, to state-licensed marijuana dispensaries instead of gas stations and convenience stores.
I do not have a problem with this. Ohio already requires licensing and oversight for intoxicating substances like alcohol. You cannot sell vodka without a license. The idea that THC products should be sold with no meaningful regulation never made sense, especially when many of these products are packaged to look like candy and are easily accessible to minors.
2. **THC caps on marijuana flower**

The bill caps THC concentration in flower at thirty five percent.
I do not see this as unreasonable. Modern marijuana is significantly stronger than it was decades ago. This is not prohibition. This is basic consumer safety and standardization.
3. **THC caps on concentrates and extracts**

The bill caps THC concentration in extracts at seventy percent.
Again, this is not outrageous. Highly concentrated products can create serious health risks, especially for inexperienced users. I do not view this as government overreach.
4. **Ban on importing marijuana from other states**

This provision makes it illegal under Ohio law to possess recreational marijuana purchased out of state, even if it was legally purchased elsewhere.
This is one of the worst parts of the bill. It criminalizes behavior that is otherwise legal solely to protect in state distribution monopolies. This is regulatory capture, not public safety. It turns ordinary people into criminals for crossing a state line with a legal product. This provision should be removed.
5. **Requirement that marijuana remain in original packaging**

Recreational marijuana must stay in its original, labeled packaging.
I have no issue with this. Alcohol, pharmaceuticals, and tobacco are all sold this way. Transparency matters. Consumers should know what they are buying.
6. **Prohibition on public consumption**

Public consumption, particularly smoking, is prohibited.
I strongly support this. I do not want to walk through clouds of weed smoke on public streets. I do not want children exposed to it. I do not want drivers smoking in cars. Legalization does not mean public nuisance is acceptable.
7. **Home grow limits maintained**

The bill maintains the existing home grow limits of six plants per adult and twelve per household.
Nothing changed here. No issue.
8. **Advertising restrictions to protect minors**

The bill restricts marketing and sales practices for intoxicating hemp products that appeal to minors.
This should have been in the original resolution. Marijuana does not need to be marketed like beer commercials with sex appeal and lifestyle imagery. If people want it, they will find it.
9. **Mandatory child resistant packaging**

Products must use child resistant packaging and clear labeling.
This is basic safety. We require it for over the counter medicine. Gummies are especially dangerous for children.
10. **Product testing and safety standards**

All products must meet testing requirements for potency, contaminants, and safety.
This is non controversial. People should know what they are consuming.
11. **Expanded licensing requirements**

The bill adds licensing and compliance requirements around intoxicating hemp products by confining them to licensed marijuana dispensaries.
This is where I start to worry. Licensing can quickly become a barrier that favors large operators with lawyers and capital. If this gets too onerous, it will crush small businesses.
12. **Background checks for license holders**

License holders are subject to background checks.
This depends on how narrowly it is applied. I do not support vague or overly punitive exclusions that permanently block people from participating in a legal industry.
13. **Penalties for non compliance**

The bill establishes penalties for violations of the regulatory framework.
This needs careful scrutiny. Enforcement should focus on safety and fraud, not turning technical mistakes into criminal acts.
14. **Track and trace supply chain monitoring**

Ohio's regulated marijuana system uses seed to sale tracking, and this framework continues.
This is common in regulated industries. Alcohol and pharmaceuticals already do this. The concern is cost and complexity, not the concept itself.
15. **Local opt out authority for dispensaries**

Adult use sales run through existing licensed dispensaries, and local governments still retain their usual zoning and permitting leverage, but this bill does not clearly create a new blanket local opt out system. If you want your local government to do something different then vote for different mayors and city council members.
16. **Tax revenue allocation to host communities**

Local governments that host dispensaries receive a share of the recreational marijuana tax revenue through the host community fund structure.
I need more transparency on how this is structured, but the concept itself makes sense.
17. **Regulatory review and future adjustments**

The framework includes mechanisms for future regulatory review. Fine, as long as it does not become an excuse for endless restriction.
The Real Problems Are Clear
Most of these provisions are not insane. Many are common sense. The real issues are these:
One, making the industry so complex that only large corporations can survive.
Two, criminalizing cross border possession for seemingly protectionist reasons.
Three, pretending voters approved something more permanent than they actually did.
People wanted legalization. They got legalization. What they did not get was immunity from legislative meddling because they chose speed over structure.
This outcome was predictable. And pretending otherwise is dishonest.
If we want durable policy, we need constitutional clarity.
That is the lesson here.
* * *

**A Final Note**
Those are my thoughts from the week. If you disagree with any of these takes please - Reply. Disagree. Yell at me if you want. That is how this is supposed to work.
* * *
Matt Diemer is an **ANGRY DEMOCRAT**. Support him by becoming a paid subscriber and sharing his newsletters.

name: Haley Stevens
subject: Grocery prices are too damn high
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
Your weekly grocery bill shouldn't feel like a car payment

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9oYWxleWZvcm1pLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.cnTsi7Aj-ckMXjfnEIKurkWwFOHPTMl-hwuSjI47Fgc?) for more
---
# [Grocery prices are too damn high](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6380051&post_id=181387646&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxMzg3NjQ2LCJpYXQiOjE3NjU3MjE0OTIsImV4cCI6MTc2ODMxMzQ5MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTYzODAwNTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.nW5VUsfKpGRNxRXPttYRfuUTllHFuVHr-8m0ztBbCsY)
### Your weekly grocery bill shouldn't feel like a car payment
| | [Haley Stevens](https://substack.com/@haleyformi)
---
| Dec 14
---
| [](https://substack.com/@haleyformi)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack..HEsqj-p_XrEVi8zQDkoC9cyoteoGisJevq5cK4aYTkI?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/haleyformi/p/grocery-prices-are-too-damn-high?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

**Happy Sunday.** You might be watching the Lions today, heading to services, or maybe you're heading out for what's become an increasingly expensive weekly ritual: grocery shopping.
If you're anything like me - or the Michiganders I talk to every day - you've noticed that grocery prices are out of control. The total at the register can feel more like a car payment than a few bags of bread, eggs, and bananas.
Let's be honest: **Grocery prices are too damn high.**
And while there are a lot of reasons behind rising costs, what's one of the biggest? **Donald Trump 's chaotic tariffs.**
[](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/c94036f1-d0a4-46f7-989b-a5ed608b4e9e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Folks, Trump's agenda isn't full of targeted, thoughtful policies. These tariffs are shoot-from-the-hip, change-by-the-minute, politics-first decisions that hurt real people and raise prices on everything from apples to eggs to coffee.
Over the last few months, I've crisscrossed Michigan - from senior centers in the U.P. to union halls in Detroit - and I hear the same thing everywhere: Folks are feeling squeezed, and no one in Washington is doing anything to help.
_So I decided to do something._
I introduced the **No Tariffs on Groceries Act** , a bill that stops presidents from unilaterally slapping tariffs on food and agricultural products without approval from Congress.
This bill is simple: If a president wants to hike prices on your groceries, they should have to explain it to Congress - and get a vote.
No more impulsive moves that reward political donors and punish working families. No more backroom deals that make your weekly grocery trip even more expensive. Just transparency, accountability, and a focus on lowering costs.
**Because here 's the reality: Trump's tariffs and reckless policies have raised prices on food staples that Michigan families rely on:**
🥩 Beef is up 16.6%
🍳 Eggs are up 10.9%
📈 Coffee, apples, bananas, bacon - all up.
That's what a chaotic trade policy looks like in real life.
I'm fighting back with the No Tariffs on Groceries Act because people deserve better. They deserve leaders who show up, listen, and deliver.
That's why I'm running for U.S. Senate - because Michigan families deserve someone who won't just _talk_ about lowering costs, but will actually do something about it.
If you're feeling the squeeze at the grocery store, please know: **you 're not alone.** I'm in this fight for you.
And if you're in a position to chip in a few dollars to support this grassroots campaign -- and our fight to make life more affordable -- _I 'd be incredibly grateful._ Every dollar helps us reach voters, fight back, and keep Michigan blue.
[Donate](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/7bc61fc2-053f-40b8-b096-0aa75d1fbb93?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
With grit and gratitude,
**Haley**
* * *

A personal note from Haley 👋

I'm a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. I've spent my career fighting for Michigan's working families - and now I'm running for the U.S. Senate to keep standing up for the people I love.

But here's the truth: Senate control could come down to Michigan. And Trump and his megadonors know it. That's why they're gearing up to spend tens of millions to defeat me and hand this seat to a MAGA Republican.

I need your help to fight back. This race will be one of the most competitive - and expensive - in the country, and I'm counting on grassroots support to win.

If you're with me, will you chip in today?
[Donate](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/7bc61fc2-053f-40b8-b096-0aa75d1fbb93?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
###### Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate.

---
| | | [Restack](https://substack..HEsqj-p_XrEVi8zQDkoC9cyoteoGisJevq5cK4aYTkI?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---

(C) 2025 Haley for MI
33717 Woodward Ave #539, Birmingham, MI 48009
[Unsubscribe](https://substack..tTMs7PzTDHoiecCHtDE5k7sQk06VVySgLd-0FXc2VyY?)
[](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/eaba05b3-6388-4902-b4a4-1d7b9329bbb3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[](https://substack..2SWuBm5m2ObqAikFnNQHDqrS5THbkhz-JwRxrBxLNHk?)

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
It's not a movie. It's Minnesota under Tim Walz and lieutenant governor Peggy Flanagan. And it's enraging. | [](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=eb3db236695a434bbf8a0a7d50288cbf&_e=RGhPA6K-U06TQ1PaD8xte10KTVpAIWQRXKcG3cWSU10Bb1T5R3xEJ9W7ApBWCq_Psi_zJ0lDY2G4qsl4uwRSuTlRuZJw3GiltLVLjnfAb9k8GHNYguY-ehoBox8M_pk9iKxVhDRkcyF0EvcR-QHaSwHA-XUdvaRGIp8gQtG0FVNYMFrKERB_Ppm4AiWk_8GVlG3NXa8WdhKII45eGOxa6KAzgJpCbvjAwAO0lVraa-D5eArA-X0Jl-wqJJA6eJMBpoodbtDQpgTjT21lmgMRKqKKdjFZt1en4QhC-u3VYwL2yd8-obThtktnuHGnl9mn8jmHTm9KL3lwMcsZvGW2F5CZXzTDpFaPA645LaFPKTDHfqucthymgMSG-LDTf_75sUqJsrzmQMnJVBZQN6Bc_Z9ZxjEb8p8q_rWso9YcGmwPcyaep29yJEZ4VNRfOIiDEC3CMfcVBoVXdhL-30PmQa7Z7XPf07p1J29wOHJLgwx6PIaXIA1Pjk6cnTs01r77QxrvWTF6OhSkpPwI0GZmRqpXaZQZ_EdAqcLJi4zxwWNPAAIP6GOwEQLj8zsyQlZwJeVn_JUvawQkiQXdfwGA_OvyNniUqfewa8pK8tZHJhftyuAD-mjG2XBw9PjZsPl82AeIS7y42n7OW_QioA4D9NduWF1fbEJJxE7JjeXMC1Vx_RaMJ5HIrPvbVofCGJsU8MQakS-CBCFcf9A1pPYELrZI8z8Uqh-cbvY-9ojh6R4Venjz7885EUtG4jV9V2fjcvjPKO9XYWhkArPHVWkEig%3D%3D)

---
Fellow Conservative,

Picture this: Your hard-earned tax dollars—meant to feed starving kids, house the homeless, and help autistic families in our backyard—stolen by the billions. Not by faceless crooks, but by a network of Somali fraudsters, Tim Walz welcomed with open arms, some wiring cash straight to al-Shabaab terrorists plotting against America.

It's not a movie. It's Minnesota under Tim Walz and Lieutenant Governor Peggy Flanagan. **And it's enraging.**

The New York Times dropped the bomb: "How Fraud Swamped Minnesota’s Social Services System on Tim Walz’s Watch." Over $1 billion vanished—more than our entire corrections budget—in brazen schemes: ghost kids "fed" in fake meal programs, phantom therapy sessions for non-existent patients, sham housing for the invisible homeless. 78 of 86 charged so far are tied to the Somali immigrant community Walz and Flanagan have shielded.

**Worse?** Whistleblowers—**over 400 brave DHS employees** —begged Walz to act years ago. Red flags everywhere. His response? _**Retaliation. Monitoring their emails. Threats to their jobs.**_ Discrediting their reports to avoid "racism" smears and keep those precious Somali votes flowing. One employee put it plain: "Walz is 100% responsible... We got the full weight of retaliation."

President Trump saw it coming.

But Walz? He gaslights on Meet the Press: "_**I take responsibility for putting people in jail.**_ "

Federal prosecutors did that, Tim. Not you.

**This is Democratic leadership:** Soft on crime, addicted to power. They audition for D.C. jobs while our state bleeds—kids go hungry, families lose homes, and terrorists get richer. **If it can happen here in the heartland, it'll devour America.**

I’m done watching from the sidelines. My name is Adam Schwarze - I’m a retired Navy SEAL, and I’m ready to serve the people of Minnesota and Americans around the nation in the United States Senate.

I'll haul Walz in for hearings, help identify every stolen cent, and slam the door on this madness.

Strong borders. Ironclad audits. Republican muscle in Washington to protect what's rightfully ours.

_**[But I need you. Chip in $25, $50, or $100 today—help me put an end to Democrat fraud and incompetence.](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=eb3db236695a434bbf8a0a7d50288cbf&_e=RGhPA6K-U06TQ1PaD8xte10KTVpAIWQRXKcG3cWSU10Bb1T5R3xEJ9W7ApBWCq_Psi_zJ0lDY2G4qsl4uwRSuTlRuZJw3GiltLVLjnfAb9k8GHNYguY-ehoBox8M_pk9iKxVhDRkcyF0EvcR-QHaSwHA-XUdvaRGIp8gQtG0FVNYMFrKERB_Ppm4AiWk_8GVlG3NXa8WdhKII45eGOxa6KAzgJpCbvjAwAO0lVraa-D5eArA-X0Jl-wqJJA6eJMBpoodbtDQpgTjT21lmgMRKgBXxfv5UB_YWzpc2iBQhhwNTPbTH9xeXzoHC-A3_A1yl-m6zP0-cGmDEzWBGzJ3fMBARr0vOBdxvrvpJSfVRFm0m8dU2iYHwDVVv8l29vNEM4q1j9AJqLkVO47SK33maJvnVBgwyWZMf5dSsDu8WoNcYd4k8WKoJyk3ih2daTLJYu8GxkoQIrsTEsTPD53pjSLnoexK1Pw6Y622uoc9ih3WG32uFP72x-wl9gxuPRtkgh_yVHHxgqTchLdfT-el6KycPx0uC2qLX0Xpnj11OuZft4qL_HifExdqBlqShcXRgynElXsdfewC3wOuTJHj9KspBHtko9mb_bVly2PI03_d91rWwPTUudSVinUwLEzrjXWT9EYqfkPktgc9U9tFu6HFCcqru5XJwmj_39jCaIj8KuoNKQ3HSN7i33iom-J02oS-p5g7b2yOsT957EJGx0F04g25eOt8IbZNGzThmgKAVaN94cBP0n5Ye9WueTdOsICrVxIsWSjR5ZtrZ74Vbw%3D%3D) **_

clean_body: It's not a movie. It's Minnesota under Tim Walz and lieutenant governor Peggy Flanagan. And it's enraging. | Fellow Conservative, Picture this: Your hard-earned tax dollars—meant to feed starving kids, house the homeless, and help autistic families in our backyard—stolen by the billions. Not by faceless crooks, but by a network of Somali fraudsters, Tim Walz welcomed with open arms, some wiring cash straight to al-Shabaab terrorists plotting against America. It's not a movie. It's Minnesota under Tim Walz and Lieutenant Governor Peggy Flanagan. And it's enraging. The New York Times dropped the bomb: "How Fraud Swamped Minnesota’s Social Services System on Tim Walz’s Watch." Over $1 billion vanished—more than our entire corrections budget—in brazen schemes: ghost kids "fed" in fake meal programs, phantom therapy sessions for non-existent patients, sham housing for the invisible homeless. 78 of 86 charged so far are tied to the Somali immigrant community Walz and Flanagan have shielded. Worse? Whistleblowers—over 400 brave DHS employees —begged Walz to act years ago. Red flags everywhere. His response? Retaliation. Monitoring their emails. Threats to their jobs. Discrediting their reports to avoid "racism" smears and keep those precious Somali votes flowing. One employee put it plain: "Walz is 100% responsible... We got the full weight of retaliation." President Trump saw it coming. But Walz? He gaslights on Meet the Press: "I take responsibility for putting people in jail. " Federal prosecutors did that, Tim. Not you. This is Democratic leadership: Soft on crime, addicted to power. They audition for D.C. jobs while our state bleeds—kids go hungry, families lose homes, and terrorists get richer. If it can happen here in the heartland, it'll devour America. I’m done watching from the sidelines. My name is Adam Schwarze - I’m a retired Navy SEAL, and I’m ready to serve the people of Minnesota and Americans around the nation in the United States Senate. I'll haul Walz in for hearings, help identify every stolen cent, and slam the door on this madness. Strong borders. Ironclad audits. Republican muscle in Washington to protect what's rightfully ours. For Minnesota. For America. For the kids they robbed. Adam Schwarze Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota Former Navy SEAL officer Marine Corps Veteran Proud Minnesotan FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

body:
Will you help keep Michigan blue?

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1642%2E49239%2EQq8Qr7)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10453?t=2&akid=1642%2E49239%2EQq8Qr7)
---
Hi null, it's Haley writing to you with a very straightforward question:

**[Is there anything I can say to convince you to donate $5 – before my crucial mid-month fundraising deadline – to help me win Michigan's battleground Senate race?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10454?t=3&akid=1642%2E49239%2EQq8Qr7)**
What if I told you that there is no path to flipping the Senate blue unless we protect Michigan's Senate seat?
What if I told you that I'm statistically tied with my Trump-backed opponent, Mike Rogers, among independent voters – and that your gift could help me surge ahead in the polls?

What if I told you I was born and raised in Michigan, and I’m in this fight for the Michiganders I know and love, not billionaire donors like the ones backing my GOP opponent?

What if I told you that national Republicans and their dark-money super PACs have already started pouring money into Michigan, and that the worst is yet to come?

What if I told you Trump and his MAGA Republicans could eclipse $80 million in spending, which is what they spent last time trying to flip Michigan red?
Null, I can win this race. But only with committed grassroots supporters like you fighting alongside me. That's why hitting my $175,000 mid-month fundraising goal – one donation at a time – is absolutely crucial.

**[So will you please chip in $5 to help me win in Michigan, take back the Senate for Democrats, and put a stop to Trump's chaos agenda?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10454?t=4&akid=1642%2E49239%2EQq8Qr7)**
| | [Donate](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10455?t=5&akid=1642%2E49239%2EQq8Qr7)
---
With grit and gratitude,

Haley
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10456?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=6&akid=1642%2E49239%2EQq8Qr7) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10453?t=7&akid=1642%2E49239%2EQq8Qr7)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley

clean_body: Would you really leave this real 600% match unused? Would you really leave this real 600% match unused? | Patriot, Ever since he left the White House, Barack Obama has been looking for an “heir” to his political movement who will continue pushing his Far Left, socialist vision for America. And now, Obama and the entire Democrat Party appear to have embraced their “ NEXT Obama” in Zohran Mamdani. They’ve caved to and endorsed Mamdani’s communist agenda, they’ve treated him like the NEW LEADER of their party, and they’ve even directed MILLIONS of dollars from their liberal donor network his way. Patriot, do you remember how many Republicans brushed off the rise of a relatively unknown liberal activist from Chicago? But knowing what we know today, no one would have taken Obama’s rise so lightly. That’s exactly why I’m imploring you now NOT to brush off the rise of Zohran Mamdani. As it stands, there’s only one check left at our disposal to stop Mamdani’s rise... ...We must FIRE Kathy Hochul – the worst governor in America – and give the state of New York its FINAL FIREWALL against Mamdani! And here’s the good news: We’re polling within the margin of error against Hochul, she’s been labeled the most vulnerable governor in the country, and this race is TIED! What's more, we have one of the greatest tools at our disposal to WIN this race. In fact, it’s the very same secret weapon that Mamdani used to win the New York mayoral race. It’s time to beat the Left at their own game! You see, the State of New York unlocks matching funds at a rate of 6-to-1 once a candidate hits the fundraising benchmarks written into state law. And with your help today, we can turn Mamdani’s secret weapon against his puppet governor Kathy Hochul by unlocking the LEGAL MAXIMUM amount of matching funds to FIRE her! Hochul and her allies know her only hope of winning reelection is to BUY the governor’s race, so when we unlock this REAL 600% MATCH, they’re going to see it as a devastating setback. Just think about it... Kathy Hochul will wake up one morning to discover that her own administration has to pay out a 600% match on thousands of grassroots contributions to FIRE her from office! So, I’ll ask again... Knowing what you know now, would you really leave this REAL 600% MATCH unused? Thank you, Elise Stefanik Paid for by Elise for Governor PO Box 500 Glens Falls, NY 12801

name: Jon Ossoff for GA
subject: What we had hoped and planned for
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

Good morning, Peter! We’ve got a bit of a problem, and we’re hoping you can lend a hand.
Here’s what’s going on:
Our fundraising so far in December is far behind what we had hoped and planned for.
It’s nothing to panic about yet, but we have to turn things around, especially because we are very quickly approaching our FEC deadline. **So we’re launching a mid-month fundraising drive to get us back on track.**
This is a critical moment for our campaign. Very soon, it will be an election year and all eyes are going to be on Georgia.
It is so important that we hit our fundraising goals and start 2026 in a strong position. Because MAGA and their Super PACs are going to throw everything they’ve got at us.
If we come up short now, we risk being unable to respond to their attacks and letting the GOP set the terms of the race.
That’s why our December fundraising is so important. It informs how all of 2026 will go, and the spending decisions we can or can’t make.
If everyone reading this pitched in $5, we’d hit our goal in less than an hour. So please:
[**Can you make a $5 contribution to Jon Ossoff’s reelection campaign today? We know not everyone can afford to give, so if you can, it makes your donation that much more important.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251214-1-full-b&t=1&akid=1271%2E5691347%2EanICro)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[CONTRIBUTE $5](https://act.electjon.com/go/1686?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.zuSSTi&refcode=em251214-1-full-b&t=20&akid=1271%2E5691347%2EanICro)**
---
****
| **[CONTRIBUTE $25](https://act.electjon.com/go/1526?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.3llSsh&refcode=em251214-1-full-b&t=21&akid=1271%2E5691347%2EanICro)**
---
****
| | **[CONTRIBUTE $50](https://act.electjon.com/go/1520?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.bGqFYH&refcode=em251214-1-full-b&t=22&akid=1271%2E5691347%2EanICro)**
---
****
| **[CONTRIBUTE $100](https://act.electjon.com/go/1521?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.IZ7DFn&refcode=em251214-1-full-b&t=23&akid=1271%2E5691347%2EanICro)**
---
****
| | **[CONTRIBUTE $250](https://act.electjon.com/go/1522?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.pWvP31&refcode=em251214-1-full-b&t=24&akid=1271%2E5691347%2EanICro)**
---
****
| **[Another Amount](https://act.electjon.com/go/34?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.PyZHDd&refcode=em251214-1-full-b&t=25&akid=1271%2E5691347%2EanICro)**

****
Thank you in advance, Peter.
In a battleground state like Georgia, every person who steps up to support our campaign makes a BIG difference.
Team Ossoff

name: Kathryn Harvey
subject: 🗳️Meet Steve Evered for Senate today at 5pm - Westside
body:
📣Vote early through Dec 19 at McCalister Square & 366 N Church St.

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/a110e40d9cb6/kathryn-harvey-for-congresscampaign-kickoff-6423287?e=edbde6c9b1)
---
|
---
|
---
| | We have had more mass shootings in America than days of the year after the mass shootings at Brown University & Australia’s Bondi Beach yesterday. If you want to get involved in gun violence prevention efforts please consider joining the**** local group[**Stop the Violence Coalition in Spartanburg**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=1c0ed0caef&e=edbde6c9b1)
---
| |
# Meet Steve Evered Write-in Candidate for the December 23 Special election for Senate TODAY at 5pm on the Westside of Spartanburg
---
| |

Steve Evered is challenging the most extreme Senator the Upstate has seen in recent memory, [Lee Bright,](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=9744fe74c7&e=edbde6c9b1) in the December 23 Special Election for Senate 12 as a write-in candidate. Senate 12 covers West Spartanburg, Reidville, Greer, East Greenville, Lyman and more. **Meet Steve today**[**Sunday, December 14 at 5:00pm in Spartanburg on the Westside**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=abbe0f13e1&e=edbde6c9b1)**.**

---
[|
---](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=ca05315287&e=edbde6c9b1)
| |
Lee Bright won the Republican Primary in October with 2500 votes. Steve is the former VP of Government Affairs for Michelin and he has come out of retirement to beat back Lee Bright who served from 2008-2016. Lee Bright fought to keep the confederate flag at the state house, sponsored extreme anti-abortion bills without exceptions, and advocated against the Affordable Care Act. We can’t afford for him to go back down to Columbia and expand the Freedom Caucus.
---
| [Support Steve ](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=bb2213aaa2&e=edbde6c9b1)
---
|
---
| |
# Support Steve before election day!
---
| |
We have several opportunities to support Steve before election day.
* **You can sign up to**[**phone bank here**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=11b21fff99&e=edbde6c9b1)**with the SPCDP on December 17**
* [**Knock doors with us**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=3e9ddf96cd&e=edbde6c9b1)**starting today at noon in Greer at the Cartwright Food Hall.**
* **Meet Steve**[**December 18 at 6:00pm at the Rights & Empowerment Art Show**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=9f1bebb752&e=edbde6c9b1)**.**

---
[|
---](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=8fab8a2ada&e=edbde6c9b1)
|
---
| |
# [You can find early voting & election day voting locations here.](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=60623ca59a&e=edbde6c9b1)
---
| | Write-in instructions are below. Early voting takes place through December 19 at the County Admin Building in Spartanburg 366 N Church Street until 5pm. Please vote early!

* [**December 23 Senate 12 Special Election at these locations 7am-7pm**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=f29f23b6b9&e=edbde6c9b1)

[ Miami elects first female democratic mayor in 30 years ](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=c4079bac41&e=edbde6c9b1)[Governor McMaster Poll shows voters weary of stricter abortion laws and rapid growth](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=4c5f520e96&e=edbde6c9b1)[Two veterans will face off in January for SC Special Elections](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=f81636845d&e=edbde6c9b1)[SC is seeking 1 Billion for Rural Healthcare here’s what the state wants to do with it](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=3b820720b7&e=edbde6c9b1)[30 new cases reported in Upstate Measles Outbreak](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=eddf243e8b&e=edbde6c9b1)

| |

# Trump launches affordability tour
---
| |

[Trump mocks affordability, doubles down on tariffs and claims voters should give up certain products like pencils.](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=8994e4af96&e=edbde6c9b1)[Nancy Mace Op-ed on frustration with Republican Party & Congress](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=9d74e51d0a&e=edbde6c9b1)

---
| | | | |
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| [|
---](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=db256bf779&e=edbde6c9b1)
| |
Last Monday we hosted candidates from across the state who are running in 2026 including US Senate, Governor, Superintendent of Education and Congress. We celebrated 2025 wins and launched Steve’s campaign for SC Senate 12!
Councilwoman Meghan Smith and I knocked doors in her district with Steve this past weekend and folks are getting excited about the Dec 23rd election. We visited with the Greenville Dem Women at their annual Holiday Party and they are fired up as well.
And finally, my good friend Tina Belge was sworn in to the Greenville City Council on Monday and they were able to hold their democratic majority because of her win. Save the date for January 27 when Octavia is sworn in for Commissioner of Public Works here in Spartanburg!
Democracy is a participatory sport, stay with me y’all.
---
| | | | | | | | | | |

We’re still in SHOCK team... this news comes after multiple other networks have bent the knee to Trump and his MAGA allies this past year!

**REMEMBER:** _ABC_ sidelined Jimmy Kimmel for making comments that MAGA disagreed with.

**AND REMEMBER:** _NBC_ is now scrubbing _MSNBC_ from its brand – despite _MSNBC_ being a critical network millions trust to hold Republicans accountable.

We can’t afford to lose one of the few remaining progressive voices on national television, but we’re nervous that Democrats like you, team, are going to tune out after this shakeup.

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

****RFK Jr just set Washington on fire with a warning bigger than the Epstein files.** **
He was called a conspiracy theorist for the COVID lab leak. Mocked about microplastics. Brushed off on FDA corruption. But he was right—every single time.

Now, fresh leaked documents reveal a coverup targeting America’s seniors: mysterious illnesses, sudden collapses explained away as “normal aging,” while hidden risks multiply in silence.
| [ ](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=c96c5b839eab41e588d6241d3f490d40&_e=Lb3x9SwmIH4JPwlgow8-G_vvoYJGtb9G3g6QxjPM5_TovBWtIBWa56g_C8kKK1XxuP5TdMjgvwYDaSmyWHcJ0arLzNvDwYL12k8imL19wPauPDhydvAWt4zPhwub0VQQsTGEQzdCdcPJBj-tGKP5g3sWFt8ARRxLAykYleIeFf6XyzvHKJ8dkCas2l1ihD4JLjbF6wbwJ76s6aK8aoVduTxU0Cd7hK-G6KaCazTr5OnXGq5Defbs2TircIvGJTbfBPooyD9axur8FwEiBxIs9Ef1jfb_KKYTbAl2PxcUH0AaszIV4mRdMfojyz5yDDhok5ox2I_8Tr4uJYpFO2hNQ_eyRe7dTbafJfcWFJxOPeKQ79b1X8a4TiAM4gKW3X_YfCnbpHrY795bOD1KH5HWhIwdAlCQ_zlyKafoKPf_X5JmvOyBj6sEJB95ivXsTkDs2X1h-XcN7p9hhVZhL6KfGYqiTbJMgOPoyXXfUXLFSQ_l22-pi2XgrORFP7tSPOKA89xEHmdSo7XwmDBtVVodpnQ388Lp2Hg6ehVgkxdnjH_Ut2eYWDJ8zVIyLue7ghJ3j8yoKzRVNgN2zhuuooJPvA%3D%3D)
---
If you were born before January 1, 1965, this is about you—and the facts Washington fears most.

[Watch RFK Jr’s urgent 2025 reveal before it disappears.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=c96c5b839eab41e588d6241d3f490d40&_e=Lb3x9SwmIH4JPwlgow8-G_vvoYJGtb9G3g6QxjPM5_TovBWtIBWa56g_C8kKK1XxuP5TdMjgvwYDaSmyWHcJ0arLzNvDwYL12k8imL19wPauPDhydvAWt4zPhwub0VQQsTGEQzdCdcPJBj-tGKP5g3sWFt8ARRxLAykYleIeFf6XyzvHKJ8dkCas2l1ihD4JEiF2mw1tyBQzmWxYG99FrID7w61wrN5vi-ALIQakSGwx4qo40rIMbIri9dX3isp1KaL-CM_MoQaIi3JuUVw4VQcMk_ziGQp2UtkMCj_u8jnyrEVKGOskp1gsxJITPLCct2CZwCtfMgdbBix0TpG_u4OsvVKzh_J40GdTdPJBBiiqtyHC1QGG_HF1K2jlBf-jEHJZSJCCuMQt5xq_NPKBT6KxFIhgh368oYeGU7XOah59eLpxvaJWpHVSvOyq9qwrmeJn3aHsZRaoriEWJjbsvkmyh-bb6LJRg33Vl6sczV_S6IOOiyKT6zIzf39Me9IbkJJ3Ra7MAmokzcpbKpdoIENK5xU60k7z_nwiCsO6U2IKCBvGCASZ8MA51pxg2FZlIM9e4tWhuZ-RFflZ5g6D9Q%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. | | | **RFK Jr just set Washington on fire with a warning bigger than the Epstein files. He was called a conspiracy theorist for the COVID lab leak. Mocked about microplastics. Brushed off on FDA corruption. But he was right—every single time. Now, fresh leaked documents reveal a coverup targeting America’s seniors: mysterious illnesses, sudden collapses explained away as “normal aging,” while hidden risks multiply in silence. | If you were born before January 1, 1965, this is about you—and the facts Washington fears most. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. body:
Normally when I write to you via email, I like them to be different from the other messages you get from most elected officials.
Peter —
Normally when you see my name in your inbox, I like the messages to be different from the other emails you get from most elected officials.
I've written to you in detail about the depth of corruption and self-enrichment of the Trump Administration, the President's thirst for political retribution and intimidation, along with other things I am seeing or working on in Washington, D.C. and at home in Georgia.
This message is different. It's more direct.
I am reaching out today with a last-ditch request for a contribution to my campaign before our mid-month fundraising drive comes to a close. Here's why:
This race will be one of the most competitive and expensive in the nation. I am the only Democratic Senator up for re-election in a state Trump won in 2024 and that makes me MAGA’s #1 target.
In 2020, the pundits said flipping a Republican Senate seat in Georgia couldn’t be done. But we built one of the strongest voter mobilization programs in Senate campaign history — and we won. That victory won the Senate majority.
Now we must win again. And we will win again.
Just like last time, I can’t do it alone.
So I’m asking:
**[Please split a contribution between my re-election campaign and Heartland Patriots today before our mid-month fundraising drive comes to a close.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98544?refcode=em20251214-9824&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9824%2E614464%2Ek7yBPF)**

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: Polly, my role model
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121256056/588860857/-510661250?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.14_Polly_B1_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzN2ZhMTA5My1mZGQ4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H_m4c1Bd0HYi88H-h8YA9XxvxlRLVUVhMkaU-RXcPk8=) Can I get a bit more personal than usual? This is a photo of me and my late grandmother, Polly. I’ve been feeling nostalgic and reflecting on why I got involved in politics — and my story starts with her. My grandmother was my first role model and my fiercest supporter. At a time when few women were involved in politics, Polly was a force – a fixture in Albany and the first person to raise their hand and volunteer. Growing up, I stuffed envelopes with Grandma Polly on weekends and slapped bumper stickers on cars — learning that civic participation is a responsibility that all of us should take seriously. She was a fiery example of what it looks like to advocate loudly for anyone without a voice. But if we’re serious about getting this done, we need this movement to step up and make sure we have the resources we need to invest big in our Senate work before our final public fundraising deadline on December 31st.
