Here’s the issue we face: Some of the voters who show up for President Trump don’t show up when he’s not on the ballot. But here’s the good news: **It doesn’t have to be that way.** We overcame it in Florida in 2022. This state was a purple battleground. Remember, we were winning by one point just a few years ago. But in 2022, we won by 1.5 million votes...a **20-point margin**. It was the biggest Republican victory in the state’s history. **How?** We didn’t run on pale pastels. We ran on **bold colors**. We drew sharp contrasts. We stuck it to the Left every single day, and we delivered real results. **If Republicans want to win, we need to give voters something to believe in, not just something to vote against.** **[That’s why I launched RON PAC.](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=18c12b3906c64ca4a3814f54b0b49380&_e=NHqFv3KhuXKd6Cc4HnkIs55TvjUm9QcJeZ2g9C_Jwuf_FPCOtRMoM094DwGKk-Q6hVY-obCUeEDCO3tnjocAwXn4ijeeKgHov8M_7mT3KpHojlbR17WxbO7Yj1G-i93fHbtsjf10AoTq1y80-jag4CH9kjccHahAyroxJZDR0u93Eyyb-K13UA1-cPIzYVPO59VhYD5IEfE_7pEHZhWbXbSDOt_iE4oYu6irOGtlwOrdEO73K_aGuk4pxJsqwrRVaHQwClkmE6dQNa8_kH_vD4VUkqxZYXRbHao5hb6Xni955P5r36a39_RbxGc4wiycunritFb8Et6eaBEVaK4WD3BXbEuUV7RY3f52qYkjrIlr_EdrzHOO9uFBUiAeyyLq5SpB2Ac2V9LOWMk-2ClszJ-EtrMwYMn1yQyHxs5TyFAz5HApJSupar0OPY7CqAwgNEqBcJOgRbci9fze0uiFBTA9GrYMG912n4jyGi1x7zXb_TK4FuWYzo_D5XXuZzClexzLcUAEoxer1B92iVqc5Sln4df2KB7_eNtFiISxO5HJcWMuL2zVcYMptXS9QT4aP7kYFfhhAPRiSiUQz-CaPylewdQr8pR0b03BeYMQhpR-B9Uu-cN9XHLL-beLPIJ4-6GhrPLxh5_WeT-mp9xrYwzhMzwsFExO2vSbdSSuh0mrqaylOoGCvsibDqa07xwusOAavfyD5s7CkePPtvkL10mrxQuSoLssfyEQQ9OAv3Y9iAG996XrIkxblZPH-Ifdv0d6LsNBNWfMeuXfgzJfzeWn3SF0jLHnbu_2hqfWa2QWMRylsSs3kHUBvZcNBtpVHjmsVuo-gH_AEaoD4Xg39w%3D%3D)** We’re electing bold conservatives across the country who don’t flinch, don’t fold, and don’t back down. clean_body: We’re electing bold conservatives across the country who don’t flinch| | | Friend, Tennessee’s special election should’ve been a slam dunk. Instead, Republicans barely held the seat, with just a 9-point win in a district President Trump carried by nearly 20. That’s a wakeup call. And if we don’t listen, we’ll lose. I went on Fox News and laid out, step-by-step, what we need to do to win in 2026. Here’s the issue we face: Some of the voters who show up for President Trump don’t show up when he’s not on the ballot. But here’s the good news: It doesn’t have to be that way. We overcame it in Florida in 2022. This state was a purple battleground. Remember, we were winning by one point just a few years ago. But in 2022, we won by 1.5 million votes...a 20-point margin. It was the biggest Republican victory in the state’s history. How? We didn’t run on pale pastels. We ran on bold colors. We drew sharp contrasts. We stuck it to the Left every single day, and we delivered real results. If Republicans want to win, we need to give voters something to believe in, not just something to vote against. We’re electing bold conservatives across the country who don’t flinch, don’t fold, and don’t back down. Leaders who will stand up for parents' rights, border security, economic sanity, and American strength, just like we’ve done in Florida. Help us take the Florida Blueprint nationwide! This moment demands action. Not next year. Not next summer. Right now. 2026 is right around the corner , and if we’re going to build a winning majority, we’ve got to get in the fight today. clean_body: today is full of noise - phony deadlines, scare tactics, and cheap gimmicks. But I want to cut through the noise for a moment. I grew up right here in Ohio. My parents worked hard to give me a chance at the American Dream, and I know what sacrifice looks like. Every opportunity I've had was built on the back of that sacrifice. why I never forget what it means when someone like you chips in a few dollars. It isn't “just money.” It 's trust. It's belief. It's a vote of confidence in the movement we're building in Ohio and beyond. So whether you 've already donated or you're considering it today, let me say this clearly: We 're in an extremely tough fight. And this isn't just any race - it's shaping up to be one of the most important in the country. I threw my hat in the ring now because we have a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to save our country. I mean that. We were from a country you and I wouldn't recognize anymore. President Trump saved us - with the help of folks like you - from that dire outcome. With that said, he needs loyal partners at the state level to help him succeed. That’s why this race means so much. So if you 've given before (even just once, even just a buck) know this: you've already made a difference, and I will never take your sacrifice for granted. And if you haven 't given yet - - I'd be honored to have you on my team. Every single gift, no matter the size, fuels this must-win fight. grateful,Vivek Ramaswamy the election were held today, we 'd come up just short.We can't afford to lose this ground -- not now, not after everything we've built.Your support right now makes the difference between a comeback and a collapse.**| | | | PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO 350 E 1st Ave Ste 100 Columbus, OH 43201

Team,

There are nine "Johns" in the Senate right now. There have only been five Black women — **_ever._**

While we're proud Lisa is among the short list of Black women who have been elected to the Senate, we're sad and frankly, disappointed, that — almost _**250 years**_ into our nation’s history — that number is still so low.

**Having people in the Senate who look like the rest of America matters.**

It's how resolutions like Black Women's Equal Pay Day, introduced by Senators Lisa Blunt Rochester and Angela Alsobrooks, get passed. Or how we make sure we advocate for Black maternal healthcare. And it's how we pass legislation honoring the late great John Lewis, expanding voting rights, and protecting people of color.

[**We need more women of color in the places where important decisions are being made. That's why we've set a goal to raise $15,000 to help elect more Black women!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121270995/588908504/-486912179?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.14_EBW-Dec-MM_B1_F1_S1_C1_U1_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYWZjMDFkMy0xOGQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pslYQc7ogDYcVImtPNhTQDgycLtvZJ3MN_QqKGOQQLU=)

**But here's a hard truth: Black women raise 1/3 of the funds their white counterparts will. That already puts us at a disadvantage we can't afford.**

[**We're urgently asking: will you chip in $10 or more today to help us elect more Black women to the Senate?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121270996/588908505/-486912179?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.14_EBW-Dec-MM_B1_F1_S1_C1_U1_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYWZjMDFkMy0xOGQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pslYQc7ogDYcVImtPNhTQDgycLtvZJ3MN_QqKGOQQLU=) | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

[👉 Click here to watch the footage and get the details.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=310424993d0d44ff954192248f2c6d1c&_e=GcvFeCyvT2CKd7Ee5DDZo9q9QwpiY4pqPoT7ITMNMHL_l-zymAIsJ-KICgAgv7kh_NjBTZ-zK_YkjlIorZrinHW4oqL8cyg1tTZug_2wvBBuZ93A_M1klcvJgQxYGxVnq21C0hZzCPCzlk9b7Ta--4YZ9ShtQBqV2EzAogG46fHcASJzbbbZu5I1K3SLwxT_-gSE2YESuKKvy-7LSCF6HbdSurP5E25pcvI_mmNY7HLeMkCsY6DHOD6r1Z8q0XmLwmpIDqDmmDLHT7isDYReTWBCWa0bsUcSuRlyXVpDe1-6v04zhsTox6FriEHOUdOygJvpDTvP267YoSimg4WpDrY-tCpjQBK-1zYzxg_A4lTLfVLordHq8-YKa0pLKdtcdB5VINWmSUW4i3-wMeEkfEaFrmLTlzGS2dB-HOV_Rdj0yrsPJnNR9AIoE5eShD3Zzfx9AuIRKJpGZjW08_M53L8XbdggsxZtKbLlgWGCt_9fBoH-32qpTQ25zb0AQ6Fi1tDmIeaziNaYn7aJpODzsB7_teUK9uQdoXqFaXpFHeFvNmsDX9cPulIF5fh6VkbsYx-p7L4nouC7wxzDQh6Hpw%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. A new demonstration video just surfaced — investors everywhere are taking notice. Elon Musk’s latest prototype didn’t just perform tasks — it learned, adapted, and exceeded predictions. The AI software making it possible isn’t actually Tesla’s own. It’s the product of a company that tech analyst Jeff Brown describes as “an overlooked leader in AI.” Their neural software now powers some of the most advanced robots — machines that recognize, react, and decide on their own. This kind of collaboration helped spur technological revolutions in the past. Now, this breakthrough supplier might be at the forefront of the next leap forward. Jeff is sharing the company’s name, symbol, and his reasons for believing it could help transform the market. Stay ahead — by the time the news spreads, this opportunity could be moving fast. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Nellie Pou
subject: Affordability is not a hoax
body:
| |
---
Hi friend, This administration might be claiming that affordability is a "hoax" or a "Democrat scam," but we both know that couldn't be further from the truth. Every time I talk to families in my district, affordability is the number one issue. Here are the facts:
* Health care premiums are going up for millions of Americans thanks to MAGA inaction on ACA tax credits.
* Holiday shopping costs went up by an average of $132.
* U.S. layoffs are on track to exceed Great Financial Crisis Levels.
* Tariffs are increasing the costs of groceries, and are forcing families to eat less and even skip meals entirely.

That is the reality working families are facing every day here in Northern New Jersey and across the country under this MAGA economy. **[To get this country back on track and get Congress back to work for working families, we must take back the House. That work starts with protecting battleground districts like mine. clean_body: | | Hi friend, This administration might be claiming that affordability is a "hoax" or a "Democrat scam," but we both know that couldn't be further from the truth. Every time I talk to families in my district, affordability is the number one issue. Here are the facts: Health care premiums are going up for millions of Americans thanks to MAGA inaction on ACA tax credits. Holiday shopping costs went up by an average of $132. U.S. layoffs are on track to exceed Great Financial Crisis Levels. Tariffs are increasing the costs of groceries, and are forcing families to eat less and even skip meals entirely. That is the reality working families are facing every day here in Northern New Jersey and across the country under this MAGA economy. = | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately. In solidarity, Nellie | | | ---|---|--- About Nellie Pou Nellie Pou is the first Latina to represent New Jersey in Congress, and will be a target for the NRCC in 2026. As a mother and grandmother, Nellie knows what New Jersey families are facing and will continue to bring down costs while creating good-paying jobs. body:
https://secure.ngpvan.com/5zGcLKjrckS5lWLLpIGsGA2?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=nBMnEzLMf+60uawdWjYbokGQeL8DIyUEX2Lq2NZK23cbOqG13S6KPssr2Y3k7j/bi14vTDPcuh8YTrCY2Qmg4vVF1jT75PpThNA06ylhd35veb0JOroTrOiXnGaS1/4P [https://secure.ngpvan.com/5zGcLKjrckS5lWLLpIGsGA2?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=nBMnEzLMf+60uawdWjYbokGQeL8DIyUEX2Lq2NZK23cbOqG13S6KPssr2Y3k7j/bi14vTDPcuh8YTrCY2Qmg4vVF1jT75PpThNA06ylhd35veb0JOroTrOiXnGaS1/4P]
Hi Peter,
OLCV volunteers lend their time and voices year after year to help us elect pro-climate candidates, and their work starts with you . Our donors are the backbone of all of our volunteers’ work of speaking to voters about OLCV endorsed candidates.
With Oregon’s Political Tax Credit, eligible tax-paying Oregonians can give $50 (individual) or $100 (couple filing jointly) to the OLCV PAC, as essentially a free donation. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/5zGcLKjrckS5lWLLpIGsGA2]
Give now [https://secure.ngpvan.com/5zGcLKjrckS5lWLLpIGsGA2?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=nBMnEzLMf+60uawdWjYbokGQeL8DIyUEX2Lq2NZK23cbOqG13S6KPssr2Y3k7j/bi14vTDPcuh8YTrCY2Qmg4vVF1jT75PpThNA06ylhd35veb0JOroTrOiXnGaS1/4P]
A previous OLCV intern, Jamie Hartmann from Portland, Oregon, wants to share their story advocating for OLCV endorsed candidates:
“I wanted a way to get involved in my community when it came to combatting climate change. When I discovered OLCV, I was astounded with the amount of effort they put in. Every Oregon congressperson's vote on every climate-related bill was recorded and factored into their "lifetime score", a measurement of how much good an Oregon politician has done in regards to pushing pro-climate bills forward and shutting anti-climate bills down. As a high school intern, I helped to canvas and phone bank during election season, write to my representatives regarding potential laws, and even got to be part of a team interviewing potential candidates for an OLCV endorsement. Simply put, OLCV does the hard work so the average climate-concerned voter has an easy guide to voting that aligns with their priorities. I was proud to work with OLCV, and my climate and political knowledge has been enriched because of it.”
We need the proper resources to win next year. Your gift using Oregon’s Political Tax Credit now will help us recruit volunteers to knock on doors and talk to voters about OLCV endorsed candidates in the 2026 election! [https://secure.ngpvan.com/5zGcLKjrckS5lWLLpIGsGA2]
Give now [https://secure.ngpvan.com/5zGcLKjrckS5lWLLpIGsGA2?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=nBMnEzLMf+60uawdWjYbokGQeL8DIyUEX2Lq2NZK23cbOqG13S6KPssr2Y3k7j/bi14vTDPcuh8YTrCY2Qmg4vVF1jT75PpThNA06ylhd35veb0JOroTrOiXnGaS1/4P]
You’re eligible for Oregon’s Political Tax Credit if:
✔ You are an Oregon resident
✔ You will submit an Oregon tax return for 2025
✔ You make less than $75,000 (individual) or $150,000 (couple filing jointly)
Thanks for your support,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AVLCV/1/70877/images/Sarah%20Signature.png]
Sarah Kline (she/they)
OLCV Communications & Membership Coordinator
P.S.: We are in the big push for end of year fundraising. If you would no longer like to receive emails related to end of year fundraising, please fill out this form here to opt-out [https://secure.everyaction.com/c2_U_ki-NEyU8Gdkttquug2] but still remain on our general email list!
https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv [https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv] https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org [https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org] https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F [https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F] https://x.com/OLCV [https://x.com/OLCV]

Oregon League of Conservation Voters
1110 SE Alder Suite 220
Portland, OR 97214
United States

body:
I want to share five reasons why a $5 contribution today would make a real difference.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121271233/588909208/800699251?refcode=BLU_EM_LGC_2025-12-14_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NzIwMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMmU0MjhjNi0xYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IY-Sb7ZVeqogTdWqpUX5XVyi9S0sNiQvrVRCBKQXV4Q=)
---
| Peter,

I want to share five reasons why a $5 contribution today would make a real difference.

**1\. This is the most competitive district in New York.**
I won by only 2 points in 2024 and I have been named the most vulnerable Democrat in the state. A small contribution goes a long way when the margins are this close.

**2\. Anthony D’Esposito is signaling he wants a rematch.**
Politico reports that he is refusing to rule out another run. He has been attacking me online for months and it is clear he is testing the waters for a comeback.

**3\. Republicans are already targeting this seat.**
This district is at the very top of their list because it could decide control of the House. Every dollar we raise now helps us stay competitive.

**4\. We cannot take back the House without winning here.**
If we lose this race, the path to a Democratic majority becomes almost impossible. That is how central this race is to the entire country.

**5\. Your contributions keep us in this fight.**
If everyone reading this email chipped in $5, we would hit our goal before the deadline.

[_**If you can give $5 (or more!) today I would be grateful. Every single contribution helps us protect this district and keep our campaign strong.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121271234/588909209/800699251?refcode=BLU_EM_LGC_2025-12-14_1&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NzIwMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMmU0MjhjNi0xYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IY-Sb7ZVeqogTdWqpUX5XVyi9S0sNiQvrVRCBKQXV4Q=)

clean_body: I want to share five reasons why a $5 contribution today would make a real difference. | = | Peter, I want to share five reasons why a $5 contribution today would make a real difference. 1\. This is the most competitive district in New York. I won by only 2 points in 2024 and I have been named the most vulnerable Democrat in the state. A small contribution goes a long way when the margins are this close. 2\. Anthony D’Esposito is signaling he wants a rematch. Politico reports that he is refusing to rule out another run. He has been attacking me online for months and it is clear he is testing the waters for a comeback. 3\. Republicans are already targeting this seat. This district is at the very top of their list because it could decide control of the House. Every dollar we raise now helps us stay competitive. 4\. We cannot take back the House without winning here. If we lose this race, the path to a Democratic majority becomes almost impossible. That is how central this race is to the entire country. 5\. Your contributions keep us in this fight. If everyone reading this email chipped in $5, we would hit our goal before the deadline. = | | | = | = | = | = | = Thank you for being part of this team. Laura * | | = Paid for and authorized by Gillen for NY. Gillen for NY PO Box 774 Rockville Centre, NY 11571 United States | = If you wish to contribute by check, you can do so at the address above.

name: Phil Weiser
subject: Lighting the menorah tonight 🕯️
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Phil Weiser for Colorado
body:
Wishing you a holiday season full of light.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121271248/588909360/1239926708?refcode=em_WEI_OTH_Hanukkah_121425&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOGMwMmJlYi0xYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2aMGNgGWTS-irLu080FltrdP6_VB3o0a5OcYrVC8Gus=&emci=dee30f62-79d7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=c8c02beb-1ad9-f011-8196-000d3a1d5215&ceid=51844520) Tonight is the first night of Hanukkah, friends. This evening, after the sun sets, my family will gather to honor the story of a miracle: the time when — against all odds — the smallest amount of oil lasted for eight nights, bringing light into the darkness in the ancient Temple. The menorah pictured above is one my grandparents buried before the Holocaust and later retrieved, passing it down to my family to celebrate the festival of lights. As we light this menorah, we celebrate the values of hope, courage, and resilience — values that kept my grandparents and mom alive during the Holocaust. The U.S. soldiers who liberated my grandmother and my mom, like the story of Hanukkah itself, brought light into the darkness. Growing up with this legacy, I grew up believing in America — the land of freedom and opportunity for all — and believing in hope, courage, and resilience, which are central to my faith. My faith teaches me that "we don’t have the obligation to complete the work of repairing our world, but we are not free to desist from doing our part." As I do my part to bring light into darkness, I am guided by the wise words of Dr. King, "darkness cannot drive out darkness, only light can do that; and hate cannot drive out hate, only love can do that." **[Friends, at its core, this movement we’re building is about hope, courage, and doing our part to repair the world. If you’re with us, I’d be honored to have your support today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121271250/588909362/-198793374?refcode=em_WEI_OTH_Hanukkah_121425&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOGMwMmJlYi0xYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2aMGNgGWTS-irLu080FltrdP6_VB3o0a5OcYrVC8Gus=)** | | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121271252/588909364/-198793374?refcode=em_WEI_OTH_Hanukkah_121425&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOGMwMmJlYi0xYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2aMGNgGWTS-irLu080FltrdP6_VB3o0a5OcYrVC8Gus=)
---
From my family to yours, chag Hanukkah sameach — happy Hanukkah — and may this holiday bring light into darkness for all of us.
— Phil
P.S. — This is also the season for delicious treats, like my grandmother’s rugelach, that I often enjoyed around Hanukkah. I hope you’ll join us for **[our upcoming virtual baking class on December 21](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121271254/588909366/1490691382?refcode=em_WEI_OTH_Hanukkah_121425&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOGMwMmJlYi0xYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2aMGNgGWTS-irLu080FltrdP6_VB3o0a5OcYrVC8Gus=)** , where I’ll share her recipe.
|

name: Fight Like Hell PAC
subject: This moment matters more than ever
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight Like Hell PAC
body:
The mid-month fundraising deadline hits tomorrow at midnight. Here's why it matters. |
#
---
Folks — is there anything we can say to earn your support before our mid-month deadline tomorrow at midnight? **What if we reminded you that this moment matters more than ever?** Democrats have a real chance to flip the House and Senate majority next year, but only if we build the strongest grassroots operation possible right now. **What if we told you that we’re already on the ground running trainings, recruiting new candidates, and building the bench of leaders who will take on extremist Republicans up and down the ballot?** This work is happening right now, and we’re counting on this team to fuel it. **And what if we said that your grassroots support helps power everything we do from launching candidate trainings to fighting back against far-right attacks?** Hundreds of thousands of grassroots supporters make up this nationwide team, but it only takes you to make a difference. While the past few months have been unlike anything we’ve seen before — in our state and in our country — the way we came together made me so proud to be an Oregonian.
When President Trump tried to use our own military against us, we showed the power of peaceful resistance.
When President Trump created a food emergency by withholding SNAP funds, Oregon stepped up to help feed our neighbors.
I’ve never been prouder to work for you — but the fight isn’t over. I’m running for Governor again because Oregon values are on the line.
The choice is between a governor who will stand up for Oregon and protect what we believe in, or a governor who will let President Trump do whatever he wants.
And with everything going on, we still have to keep building on the progress we’ve made, to make sure people can afford to call Oregon home.
We expanded the number of shelter beds, began building more homes people can afford, funded summer learning, and found ways to ease the rising cost of living.
**It’s a start, but we need to do more. Especially with everything coming at us from Washington, D.C.**
As Governor, I’ve visited all thirty-six counties across Oregon, and I’ve seen firsthand the strength and resilience that makes our state such a special place.
That’s why I’m so optimistic about our future.
**It’s why I’m excited to officially announce my re-election campaign for Governor of our great state.**

name: Sarah McBride
subject: What Franklin Roosevelt said about dictatorships
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
As World War II raged, Franklin Roosevelt talked about how necessitous people are not free people.
#
I know that a lot of times folks see what is going on in the news — the recklessness, the violations of the rule of law, the corruption — and they go, _“how on earth can you talk about affordability? Democracy is crumbling around us, and our government and our constitution are at stake. Why are you talking about the price of groceries?”_
But one of the things I know, and I see, and I hear, is that for the vast majority of people who can't make ends meet every single day, they don't have the luxury of caring about everything else.
We owe it to them to solve their problems. One, because that is the responsibility of elected officials, and two, because if we care about the survival of our democracy, **we have to understand that economic opportunity is the foundation upon which freedom flourishes.**
When I gave my first speech on the floor of the House of Representatives, I thought long and hard about what I wanted to say. And I kept coming back to a speech that had been delivered in that same well in 1944 by Franklin Roosevelt.
As World War II raged, Franklin Roosevelt talked about how necessitous people are not free people. When you are hungry and out of a job, those are the things that dictatorships are made of.
That’s why all of us who believe in democracy in this moment, who recognize that this American experiment that started 250 years ago is at risk in this moment, need to recognize that the only way to save the American experiment is to make the American dream affordable and accessible for _everyone_.
**That's why I will continue to focus on it—because my constituents demand it, they need it, but also because the future of our country requires it.**
But I’m going to need your support along the way. If you’re with me in the fight to make life affordable _and_ defend our democracy, and if you’re in a position to give, I need you to [**pitch in $5 — or whatever amount works for you — to support my campaign today.**](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20251214-1.RCISaG&refcode=em20251214-2583&amount=5&t=1&akid=2583%2E21716%2E6ZjZeg)
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20251214-1.RCISaG&amount=5&refcode=em20251214-2583&t=20&akid=2583%2E21716%2E6ZjZeg)**
In solidarity,
Sarah McBride

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

He signed a document that Wall Street executives desperately hoped would stay hidden.| | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | Something extraordinary happened just 24 hours after Trump returned to office... Can you take our final WFP member survey of the year? We value hearing from each and every one of you, and your responses this month will be especially helpful as we plan for 2026: **[](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoe1DuqQbjt4KoloTZxD-XRK680HRuv8YDi3Fg9cQWCt7LNmSWy70q2N5LQFWRQXFcTLbjmWXq0qiyeN_kCpKNOAtLvtimLKEyk573uiVBzKZBHJL16rXlk4Ns-UknJzreyRA0U1lniftRUw7U8on20cWwglZ_VlpAn_29CPOEY9PoZn07T-ltOCiugKL1mLZBKPimlgjTuiiQwidHsUkxGtwwatWfw-_Wx34Pokb8b4lLU3OQZH6OS16kb6Vez8A7oTr2cjmrc_E4G8kKzz90Zs9q1LUikc5_tYz8VpvjY2PJEywbxvZdmyEILWFLNtE-g/4mf/_TT9l2BsTJayqXt1_WJv6g/h0/h001.DUHyBJkMFQ94uQ-CuWjeyYcV27A2URpgrVRzwM6Ryek)** **[Take the survey »](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoe1DuqQbjt4KoloTZxD-XRK680HRuv8YDi3Fg9cQWCt7LNmSWy70q2N5LQFWRQXFcTLbjmWXq0qiyeN_kCpKNOAtLvtimLKEyk573uiVBzKZaMhAJ8a29E8Zf7PMAqF99vimE53HZRBxE2CKdfyZCTCBz3Hg_94SDz9Sp7AFfRI8CLYhHgSfJj7e1fJfFkHd-90M-pLcAjvqZzEmAb6elyK4jjKlopDGQaZRBd5AcU5a37prBoMk0sYbiAmOyWmLnwLZ3wzrqdxtl5aYQOLwACSy7pxgj1VWU76GGttgfIIy0sx4r3N_Ujo0pVOQ_0HPrQ/4mf/_TT9l2BsTJayqXt1_WJv6g/h1/h001.3GS084kPq6DQKfBYuI_pqqlyEeMzraqiy4yyCgb9O-4)** **As a party of, by, and for working people, your priorities guide everything we do, Peter.** And as we head into the holidays and prepare to close out 2025, we’re already looking ahead to a major election year — one that gives us the chance to shrink the GOP’s power in Congress, statehouses, and local offices across the country. Next year’s midterms are our opportunity to elect champions for working people at _every_ level of government, take back the U.S. House, end Mike Johnson’s speakership, and put a real check on Donald Trump and the MAGA movement. **But to make that happen, we need your input.** [**Take our final WFP Member Survey of the year. It should only take a few minutes, and every answer is read, valued, and shared with WFP leadership.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoe1DuqQbjt4KoloTZxD-XRK680HRuv8YDi3Fg9cQWCt7LNmSWy70q2N5LQFWRQXFcTLbjmWXq0qiyeN_kCpKNOAtLvtimLKEyk573uiVBzKZcbBkeYGQ9sS071EUnnsd9P7tu_9j7eBfY0tZ-XmvYfyTwmzKW7bsxAajLOrWu7SrVhp8tgi3u3P4KAbShog_1401yH-PAZWtaI7P0qtzIAvC4ZNGFNAgD0Z33MZwwqZR69pI_hUlJLCoUJ4GBlb9QRR7AI2Gnr2Rdhc44EGeaWnVRwtrBdi4666K7QtZJ2DGOAa5hEMgPxxN389ZvhTvHw/4mf/_TT9l2BsTJayqXt1_WJv6g/h2/h001.o5D1YsQrupNh7Zrx4-nZ2k3Y2LYUuwOse3ucycJ78qA) **[Take the survey »](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoe1DuqQbjt4KoloTZxD-XRK680HRuv8YDi3Fg9cQWCt7LNmSWy70q2N5LQFWRQXFcTLbjmWXq0qiyeN_kCpKNOAtLvtimLKEyk573uiVBzKZbUgmKqkL6fU2c_hMpysTKmWBfSHRuOyAWXwMpY9I7ac_vcnBB_vDt8vUNHUX9ZidNrUggRNWg-tGgrpuEwBREuBU_Gh8W2UK9099ZFLNDoJM7pw27sxmu62mlbweFWVKNZADBhhCar7Osh3K0MUAEa7GQxILG9iKbcZHHUtXCWJw9AoMBpftZYRJUdxCYKmysr7SvRlMPLVrvNtiBBdUGw/4mf/_TT9l2BsTJayqXt1_WJv6g/h3/h001.bkJKw1LpUz-P_bQ3iL8sbq1jsefzZ4nEcBscL1Zb5Sk)** Throughout 2025, your insights have guided WFP leadership, and helped steer real decisions — about how we organize, where we invest, and what we fight for. WFP is made up of working people, standing with working people. And your voice? It doesn’t just get heard. It’s what _drives_ this movement. **To show just how much we pay attention, here’s what you told us were your top three priorities in our last survey:**
1. Pushing back against Trump and MAGA policies
2. Electing more champions in state, local, and special elections
3. Organizing to win pro-working families policies and defend our communities

Now, it’s time to dig deeper: Which issues matter most to you right now — inflation and the rising cost of living, healthcare and prescription drug prices, protecting democracy, or something else? Which federal policies would make you cheer to see a candidate running for Congress support — a cap on rent hikes, a pathway to citizenship for undocumented immigrants, guaranteed healthcare, or another big solution for working families? **Your answers won’t just sit in a spreadsheet, Peter.** They’ll guide how we vet candidates for endorsement, decide which campaigns to support, and shape the fights we take on for working people across the country in 2026. [**Take our final WFP Member Survey of the year today — because yes, we really do read every response.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoe1DuqQbjt4KoloTZxD-XRK680HRuv8YDi3Fg9cQWCt7LNmSWy70q2N5LQFWRQXFcTLbjmWXq0qiyeN_kCpKNOAtLvtimLKEyk573uiVBzKZONW4BpyBkGOiQQRP7fwzHcu3ViJiEGvgg_DV274-yCkvhex8SPM7dXfTEgUXdj5qTFXl1zWa6vKV62zBIxTwmXAkTyy0pvaCgCGb-j7d3IgKDOvYgOpe_aR6PROMHiFMjZ7pgpzr8T9ERo8C_GsMQPD4d4TbFIuHwKfcY9FH-0MNFt-jIoP7AgMVUt7vG0FB3N47buy5i9LomTh3dOr89w/4mf/_TT9l2BsTJayqXt1_WJv6g/h4/h001.cRa3EPxhdVpg63MyeOQ0svMqyOgyIVq42mQrYjNieNw) Thank you for showing up with your time, your ideas, your energy, and your solidarity. We’re building governing power for working people, and we’re honored to fight alongside you. In gratitude, always, WFP Digital Team clean_body: | | | Can you take our final WFP member survey of the year? We value hearing from each and every one of you, and your responses this month will be especially helpful as we plan for 2026: As a party of, by, and for working people, your priorities guide everything we do, Peter. And as we head into the holidays and prepare to close out 2025, we’re already looking ahead to a major election year — one that gives us the chance to shrink the GOP’s power in Congress, statehouses, and local offices across the country. Next year’s midterms are our opportunity to elect champions for working people at every level of government, take back the U.S. House, end Mike Johnson’s speakership, and put a real check on Donald Trump and the MAGA movement. But to make that happen, we need your input. Throughout 2025, your insights have guided WFP leadership, and helped steer real decisions — about how we organize, where we invest, and what we fight for. WFP is made up of working people, standing with working people. And your voice? It doesn’t just get heard. It’s what drives this movement. To show just how much we pay attention, here’s what you told us were your top three priorities in our last survey: 1. Pushing back against Trump and MAGA policies 2. Electing more champions in state, local, and special elections 3. Organizing to win pro-working families policies and defend our communities Now, it’s time to dig deeper: Which issues matter most to you right now — inflation and the rising cost of living, healthcare and prescription drug prices, protecting democracy, or something else? Which federal policies would make you cheer to see a candidate running for Congress support — a cap on rent hikes, a pathway to citizenship for undocumented immigrants, guaranteed healthcare, or another big solution for working families? Your answers won’t just sit in a spreadsheet, Peter. They’ll guide how we vet candidates for endorsement, decide which campaigns to support, and shape the fights we take on for working people across the country in 2026. Thank you for showing up with your time, your ideas, your energy, and your solidarity. We’re building governing power for working people, and we’re honored to fight alongside you. In gratitude, always, WFP Digital Team Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: “AIPAC spent millions to keep her out of Congress. Now, she sees an opening.”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

The fight to take down AIPAC runs right through North Carolina’s 4th
congressional district, Peter.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251214-na.ooBv4O&refcode=em20251214-37277&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=37277%2E1203867%2Epc28AB ]The Intercept: AIPAC spent millions to keep her out of Congress. Now,
she sees an opening. Growing dissatisfaction with the Israel lobby may
pave a lane for Nida Allam, who launched her congressional campaign in
North Carolina Thursday with the backing of Justice Democrats.

Our newest 2026 Democratic primary candidate, Nida Allam, is running to
unseat a genocide-enabling incumbent who got to Congress with millions of
dollars of help from the Israel lobby.

Rep. Valerie Foushee’s 2022 campaign was bankrolled by anti-Palestinian
lobbies like AIPAC and the Democratic Majority for Israel (DMFI). In
return, she voted to spend BILLIONS of our taxpayer dollars on weapons and
bombs for the Israeli military.

$432 million of North Carolinians’ tax dollars went to weapons for Israel
— that money could have paid for one month’s worth of groceries for every
family in NC-04, or powered every home in the district with solar power
for a year and then some!

To make matters worse, Foushee refuses to call Israel’s actions what they
are: a genocide. She even went on an AIPAC-sponsored trip to Israel last
year and met with Benjamin Netanyahu. In the middle of a genocide, she
posed for photos with the government committing it — that’s completely
disqualifying.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251214-na.ooBv4O&refcode=em20251214-37277&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=37277%2E1203867%2Epc28AB ]Photo of Rep. Valerie Foushee with other U.S. representatives posing
with Benjamin Netanyahu

Nida, on the other hand, has been a long-time supporter of Palestinian
freedom. That’s why the Israel lobby dropped $2.5 million against her
campaign in 2022. She has remained a vocal opponent of the genocide in
Gaza and will oppose sending another cent to Israel for bombs and weapons.

This competitive primary is our first opportunity on the calendar to fight
back against AIPAC and replace one of their stooges in Congress.

Like Nida told The Intercept, “We cannot allow AIPAC and these corporate
billionaires to scare us into silence. It’s actually our mandate to take
them on directly, especially now as they’re losing their sway in the
Democratic Party.”

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251214-na.ooBv4O&refcode=em20251214-37277&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1004&akid=37277%2E1203867%2Epc28AB ]If you want to see her in Congress instead of Foushee, split a donation
between Nida’s campaign and Justice Democrats today so we can take this
seat back from AIPAC and strengthen our fight to end the genocide in Gaza.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251214-na.ooBv4O&refcode=em20251214-37277&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1023&akid=37277%2E1203867%2Epc28AB ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251214-37277-footer&t=1024&refcode2=37277_1203867_pc28AB&akid=37277%2E1203867%2Epc28AB

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1025&akid=37277%2E1203867%2Epc28AB

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster, PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121272333/588912762/-2063340125?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMzE0NmEzOS0xZmQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fAqQr9SO6ei7zLM1eDsweEOImr-Xk0a6chgtKU1L3X4=)
Friend,

It’s hard to believe, but the year is almost over -- and Election Day is closer than you think.

**There are less than 100 days until the primary election in Illinois.** My team and I are focusing on the voter outreach efforts we need to spread the word from yard signs to door knocking and everything in between.

**The end of the year also marks our final end-of-quarter deadline before the election year is in full swing.** Come January, we’re required by the FEC to report our fundraising totals -- and you can bet our opponents and national Republicans will be combing it over for any signs of weakness.

**The GOP knows that with only three seats separating House control, IL-13 could make or break the majority.** I was proud to flip this seat back in 2022 and become the first woman to ever represent this seat in Congress -- but as you can imagine, it also put me on the top of the GOP target list.

I haven’t shied away from doing the right thing for Illinois’ working families. I’m dedicated to tackling the affordability crisis, securing better healthcare, and protecting our local economies from farming to steel.

**[Your support has been crucial, and I hope I can count on you again. Will you chip in $3 to help us end the year with strength?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121272335/588912763/-2063340125?refcode=em-12.14.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMzE0NmEzOS0xZmQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fAqQr9SO6ei7zLM1eDsweEOImr-Xk0a6chgtKU1L3X4=)**

name: Congressman Mike Lawler
subject: Wishing you a blessed Chanukah
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lawler for Congress, Inc
body:
From our family to yours

| | | | [ ](https://www.lawlerforcongress.com/)
---
---
| | Peter, As families across New York and our country gather to light the menorah, I want to extend my warmest wishes for a blessed and joyful Chanukah.
This season is a reminder of the power of faith, perseverance, and light in the face of darkness. Your strength and dedication to our community continue to inspire me every day.
From my family to yours, may your Chanukah be filled with peace, health, and happiness.
---

name: JB Pritzker
subject: Look...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
This race could decide the U.S. Senate, Peter
#
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98389?refcode=em20251214-9830&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9830%2E614464%2EdJ0Jqd)
Peter — JB Pritzker here.
Let me cut to the chase: **New Hampshire’s Senate race could decide whether Democrats take back the majority — _and Republicans know it._**
Donald Trump and the MAGA GOP see this seat as one of their best shots to expand their power.
They’ve already got a Republican governor in New Hampshire, and they’re betting they can flip the Senate seat next. If they win here, our chances of taking back the Senate drop to zero.
_We can’t let that happen._
Chris Pappas is a proven fighter for working families — taking on Big Pharma, defending reproductive freedom, and making sure our government actually works for the people it serves.
I’ve seen what happens when leaders like Chris have the platform and the power to make change — and _I want him in the Senate._
I know Chris can win, but there’s no way around it: **_This isn’t a safe blue seat, folks._**
The GOP is ready to flood New Hampshire with attack ads and dark money. The only way to beat them is with early, grassroots support. That’s where you come in, Peter:
**[If you can, please split a $15 donation right now between Chris Pappas and State Democrats Committee to put Democrats on the path to a Senate majority.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98390?refcode=em20251214-9830&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=15&t=2&akid=9830%2E614464%2EdJ0Jqd)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:
[Contribute $5](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98390?refcode=em20251214-9830&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=5&t=3&akid=9830%2E614464%2EdJ0Jqd) [Contribute $15](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98390?refcode=em20251214-9830&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=15&t=4&akid=9830%2E614464%2EdJ0Jqd) [Contribute $30](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98390?refcode=em20251214-9830&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=30&t=5&akid=9830%2E614464%2EdJ0Jqd) [Contribute $50](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98390?refcode=em20251214-9830&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=50&t=6&akid=9830%2E614464%2EdJ0Jqd) [Contribute $100](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98390?refcode=em20251214-9830&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=100&t=7&akid=9830%2E614464%2EdJ0Jqd) [Contribute $250](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98390?refcode=em20251214-9830&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=250&t=8&akid=9830%2E614464%2EdJ0Jqd) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98389?refcode=em20251214-9830&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=9&akid=9830%2E614464%2EdJ0Jqd)

JB Pritzker

name: =?utf-8?B?QW5kcmVh4oCZcyBpUGhvbmU=?=
subject: An important update
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Andrea Salinas for Oregon
body:
Team, it’s Andrea. Before you log off to enjoy the weekend, I have a quick request: Can you chip in $6 to keep me in the fight for Oregon’s 6th District? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/salinas-email-q42025?amount=6]

I know your inbox has likely been full of donation requests, but this is why it’s important:

I’m fighting for YOU, not right-wing billionaires or special interests: [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/salinas-email-q42025?amount=6] I’ve voted NO every step of the way on House Republicans' cruel Medicaid and SNAP cuts. I’m sounding the alarm on Trump’s billionaire tax giveaway in the media, online, and at events across my district. And in the midst of the GOP’s destruction, I’m still working to deliver meaningful, positive change for Oregon’s working families.

But National Republicans know I’ve been a thorn in their side. [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/salinas-email-q42025?amount=6] They’ve come within single digits of flipping OR-06 in two straight elections, and we know they won’t hesitate for a moment to flood this district with dark-money attack ads to replace me with a MAGA rubber stamp. Your donation will help ensure my campaign is ready to respond to any attacks coming our way.

That’s why I’m coming to this grassroots team today: So, Team are you with me? Please chip in $6 or whatever you can to keep OR-06 blue and help me hold the line in Congress as I fight for Oregon’s working families. → [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/salinas-email-q42025?amount=6]

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

And in the midst of the GOP’s destruction, I’m still working to deliver meaningful, positive change for Oregon’s working families. But National Republicans know I’ve been a thorn in their side. name: Haley Stevens
subject: What I've been hearing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
These aren’t political talking points – they’re real concerns from real people

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1639%2E49239%2EzUz_mP)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10411?t=2&akid=1639%2E49239%2EzUz_mP)
---
Hi null,

I’ve been on the road, visiting communities all across Michigan – and everywhere I go, I hear the same things:
🩺 People are anxious about affording health care.
🛟 They’re worried about the future of Medicare and Social Security.

💰 Grocery prices are too damn high.

👷 Working Michiganders want real action to create and protect good-paying jobs.

🗳️ And many are deeply concerned about our civil rights, democracy, and basic freedoms.
These aren’t political talking points – they’re real concerns from real people. And that’s exactly why I’m running for U.S. Senate in Michigan. Because when I see a problem, I get to work:
✅ I helped save 200,000 jobs as part of President Obama’s Auto Rescue Task Force.

✅ I led the charge to bring semiconductor manufacturing back to Michigan.

✅ I introduced the No Tariffs on Groceries Act to stop reckless food tariffs that are raising grocery prices.

✅ And I filed articles of impeachment against RFK Jr. for endangering public health.
I was recently named one of the most effective lawmakers in Congress – because I pride myself on showing up, listening, and delivering for working families.

Now I’m bringing that same grit to this battleground Senate race. With polls showing I’m the Democrat best positioned to defeat MAGA Republican Mike Rogers, GOP megadonors are set to spend millions to flip this seat red.
So I’m asking: _[**Will you be one of the 15,000 grassroots supporters we need to endorse this campaign before midnight tonight? We’re still 28% short, and your name would mean a lot, null. >>**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10414?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=3&akid=1639%2E49239%2EzUz_mP)_
| | [Add Your Name](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10414?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=4&akid=1639%2E49239%2EzUz_mP)
---
Thank you for standing with me.
With grit and gratitude,

Haley Stevens
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10414?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=5&akid=1639%2E49239%2EzUz_mP) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10411?t=6&akid=1639%2E49239%2EzUz_mP)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley
