name: Aaron Regunberg from All right, then, I'll go to hell
subject: Reflections on the shooting at Brown
body:
There may be nothing new to say. I guess that's the point.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..zqGrh_Sz4F1_8CzKBNkOK40KVTnc7jGU1o3ElC_EmI0?) for more
---
# [Reflections on the shooting at Brown](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=181593825&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxNTkzODI1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjU3Mzk3NTAsImV4cCI6MTc2ODMzMTc1MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ5ODExNzAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.uUYIx9LiId81sx0bQY5_COWio1W45Mj4r7MDZTFMius)
### There may be nothing new to say. I guess that's the point.
| | [Aaron Regunberg](https://substack.com/@aaronregunberg)
---
| Dec 14
---
| [](https://substack.com/@aaronregunberg)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=181593825&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxNTkzODI1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NTczOTc1MCwiZXhwIjoxNzY4MzMxNzUwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDk4MTE3MCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.I5rqCkGo2mrGsGdSXJxIM53ymjQ0DP7CupjNTykkmV8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=181593825&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxNTkzODI1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjU3Mzk3NTAsImV4cCI6MTc2ODMzMTc1MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ5ODExNzAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.uUYIx9LiId81sx0bQY5_COWio1W45Mj4r7MDZTFMius&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=181593825&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxNTkzODI1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjU3Mzk3NTAsImV4cCI6MTc2ODMzMTc1MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ5ODExNzAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.uUYIx9LiId81sx0bQY5_COWio1W45Mj4r7MDZTFMius)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvYWFyb25yZWd1bmJlcmcvcC9yZWZsZWN0aW9ucy1vbi10aGUtc2hvb3RpbmctYXQtYnJvd24_.r-Yrhk3ZqmowQif_GBru-13qM4T1vcXJlZxaMrfBYsc?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/aaronregunberg/p/reflections-on-the-shooting-at-brown?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

I was at the playground, pushing my four-year-old son on the swing, when my wife texted me Saturday afternoon. "Are you seeing what's happening at Brown right now?" Then she sent a screenshot of the Brown University [alert](https://substack.com/redirect/9c05cb25-89f5-4335-b5ee-16139f3cd4ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): "There's an active shooter near Barus & Holley Engineering. Lock doors, silence phones and stay hidden until further notice. Remember: RUN, if you are in the affected location, evacuate safely if you can; HIDE, if evacuation is not possible, take cover; FIGHT, as a last resort, take action to protect yourself."
RUN, HIDE, FIGHT--the best shield we've seen fit to provide to our children.
I have an iron rule against letting myself doomscroll when I'm at the playground. But I started scrolling like crazy, in between swing pushes, trying to get a sense of what was happening. In the distance multiple sirens wailed. I saw a [post](https://substack.com/redirect/0aac51d5-e543-4061-a40e-f6b8ba8caf79?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from President Trump: "I have been briefed on the shooting that took place at Brown University in Rhode Island. The FBI is on the scene. The suspect is in custody." (This tweet is still, somehow, up.) Then another [alert](https://substack.com/redirect/0508e2ac-015d-4996-a4f9-5b98e6903ff8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from Brown: "Continue to shelter in place. Remain away from Barus & Holley area. Police do not have a suspect in custody and continue to search for suspect(s)." A fire truck roared by.
It occurred to me that maybe we should head home. I wasn't scared, exactly, but we live less than a mile from Brown, so we were probably included in the shelter in place order? But my four-year-old had been feeling sick earlier and spent the whole day on the couch--he really needed this playground time. So I kept pushing the swing.
There was only one other person at the park, a young woman shooting free-throws on the basketball court. Around the time of Brown's third [alert](https://substack.com/redirect/5d06612f-4d23-4439-a133-8929dc4d9b60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--"Report of shots fired near Governor street. Continue to shelter in place. Stay clear"--she started walking towards the road. She stopped as she passed us: "Hey, just so you know, there's a shooting at Brown. My friends are telling me I should get inside." I thanked her. "Okay, time to go, buddy," I told my son.
By the time we got home, there were helicopters in the sky. We went inside, made dinner, got the baby and then the kid into bed--we decided not to tell him about what was happening, not tonight--and then sat, scrolling for news, occasionally flagging an update for one another. "They haven't found him?" "Looks like students are still hiding out in buildings." "Oof, did you see there's a kid who survived Parkland, going through it all again?" And the helicopters kept circling, all night long. What use, I wondered, are helicopters for finding a man hiding in a city after sunset?
I went to sleep that night thinking about three things. First, the victims. The students who were killed. The families, the parents, excited for their children to get home for the holidays. What will be the medium by which they learn the life-shattering, never-the-same-afterwards news? And the hundreds of other students who've spent the night, hours now, hiding behind desks and under tables, waiting to be evacuated. My own Brown undergrad memories remain so meaningful to me--the big moments, meeting the woman who would become my wife; the little moments, staying up with friends that one night to see a comet, fighting to stay awake until 6 a.m., when the doors would finally open at Louis, the greasy spoon just a block and a half from Barrus & Holley. How will tonight's trauma deform these young people's memories of their college years?
Second, I thought about America. The country of that almost-mythological _[Onion](https://substack.com/redirect/3eccaa85-a26e-4d88-96de-07c7f4c92fc6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[headline](https://substack.com/redirect/3eccaa85-a26e-4d88-96de-07c7f4c92fc6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), "'No Way To Prevent This,' Says Only Nation Where This Regularly Happens." Where we can muster helicopters and [over 400](https://substack.com/redirect/1d2bc8a1-c805-402c-9eaf-7a4f450be996?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) often heavily-armed law enforcement personnel to respond to a college shooting--where our federal leaders just, days earlier, [passed](https://substack.com/redirect/599cc89b-3093-4f2f-8cdf-3947bcb4644d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a bipartisan $900 billion "defense" spending bill for Pete Hegseth's Department of War; where weapons suppliers are [making their fortunes](https://substack.com/redirect/3513f962-6736-4a27-a22b-c7f10a2f52bf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) outfitting ICE to raid U.S. cities, with $205 million in DHS orders for guns, ammunition, and chemical munitions in a single month--but we can't make the barest gesture towards prevention beforehand, beyond preemptively teeing up our RUN, HIDE, FIGHT alerts. Where the White House's official Twitter account still, to this day, displays Trump's blatantly false announcement about a shooter in custody.
But perhaps my most upsetting feeling that night--sheltering in place, manhunt underway--was tedium. This story has been seen, digested, and repeated with such regularity that it's now rote. The horrors of American gun violence are so banal that even the observation that they're banal is boring, and has been for years. For the families of the victims, today is a thunderbolt that might break them forever. For the students and others who spent hours in hiding, the trauma may be lifelong. But for everyone else, this was just the [389th mass shooting](https://substack.com/redirect/e571fe0c-6114-496b-84ad-e9fa09057f41?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in America this year. Tomorrow, or the day after, there will be a new one to overshadow it, just as this tragedy overshadowed its [predecessor](https://substack.com/redirect/28a9b799-e99e-4b96-b437-4ca978b0387d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Sometimes the process is concurrent, like the [shooting](https://substack.com/redirect/e2cfdb28-fe79-4e00-af3d-6754c3f68c29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at Evergreen High School that was immediately--simultaneously--eclipsed by the shooting of Charlie Kirk. It killed me, thinking about those kids in Colorado, seeing their governor fly their state's flags at [half mast](https://substack.com/redirect/c5cdd109-8c78-4612-b1a0-d58241febb02?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for Kirk, rather than for the nightmare that had just consumed their community. And it kills me that none of the victims from Brown will get even the tiniest fraction of the mandatory mourning--the [required](https://substack.com/redirect/1122306a-26cc-450a-b885-3c4eea02b312?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [moments of silence](https://substack.com/redirect/c5ae9d3a-28f9-4f84-87ef-fec6076d0cc8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the [Ezra Klein eulogies](https://substack.com/redirect/2b62179c-02ae-47a0-ba32-65447732a1ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the massive federal government [policy response](https://substack.com/redirect/efc53704-9c18-4df1-96c2-bbc74eae0e1e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--of that man, who [said](https://substack.com/redirect/98f128b0-4f7c-4cb7-ad02-b41cf16b4d4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) their deaths were "worth it," were just the price you pay for freedom.
Christina Paxson, the President of Brown University, said at one of the press briefings after the shooting, "This is a day one hopes never happens. And it has." At this late stage of our national death drive, that may be the only useful takeaway we can draw from these tragedies: Wherever we are, we hope it never happens in our city, our neighborhood, our school. And, eventually, it will.
* * *

clean_body: There may be nothing new to say. I guess that's the point. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more There may be nothing new to say. I guess that's the point. | | | Dec 14 | | | | | | | | | | | | I was at the playground, pushing my four-year-old son on the swing, when my wife texted me Saturday afternoon. "Are you seeing what's happening at Brown right now?" Then she sent a screenshot of the Brown University : "There's an active shooter near Barus & Holley Engineering. Lock doors, silence phones and stay hidden until further notice. Remember: RUN, if you are in the affected location, evacuate safely if you can; HIDE, if evacuation is not possible, take cover; FIGHT, as a last resort, take action to protect yourself." RUN, HIDE, FIGHT--the best shield we've seen fit to provide to our children. I have an iron rule against letting myself doomscroll when I'm at the playground. But I started scrolling like crazy, in between swing pushes, trying to get a sense of what was happening. In the distance multiple sirens wailed. I saw a from President Trump: "I have been briefed on the shooting that took place at Brown University in Rhode Island. The FBI is on the scene. The suspect is in custody." (This tweet is still, somehow, up.) Then another from Brown: "Continue to shelter in place. Remain away from Barus & Holley area. Police do not have a suspect in custody and continue to search for suspect(s)." A fire truck roared by. It occurred to me that maybe we should head home. I wasn't scared, exactly, but we live less than a mile from Brown, so we were probably included in the shelter in place order? But my four-year-old had been feeling sick earlier and spent the whole day on the couch--he really needed this playground time. So I kept pushing the swing. There was only one other person at the park, a young woman shooting free-throws on the basketball court. Around the time of Brown's third --"Report of shots fired near Governor street. Continue to shelter in place. Stay clear"--she started walking towards the road. She stopped as she passed us: "Hey, just so you know, there's a shooting at Brown. My friends are telling me I should get inside." I thanked her. "Okay, time to go, buddy," I told my son. By the time we got home, there were helicopters in the sky. We went inside, made dinner, got the baby and then the kid into bed--we decided not to tell him about what was happening, not tonight--and then sat, scrolling for news, occasionally flagging an update for one another. "They haven't found him?" "Looks like students are still hiding out in buildings." "Oof, did you see there's a kid who survived Parkland, going through it all again?" And the helicopters kept circling, all night long. What use, I wondered, are helicopters for finding a man hiding in a city after sunset? I went to sleep that night thinking about three things. First, the victims. The students who were killed. The families, the parents, excited for their children to get home for the holidays. What will be the medium by which they learn the life-shattering, never-the-same-afterwards news? And the hundreds of other students who've spent the night, hours now, hiding behind desks and under tables, waiting to be evacuated. My own Brown undergrad memories remain so meaningful to me--the big moments, meeting the woman who would become my wife; the little moments, staying up with friends that one night to see a comet, fighting to stay awake until 6 a.m., when the doors would finally open at Louis, the greasy spoon just a block and a half from Barrus & Holley. How will tonight's trauma deform these young people's memories of their college years? Second, I thought about America. The country of that almost-mythological , "'No Way To Prevent This,' Says Only Nation Where This Regularly Happens." Where we can muster helicopters and often heavily-armed law enforcement personnel to respond to a college shooting--where our federal leaders just, days earlier, a bipartisan $900 billion "defense" spending bill for Pete Hegseth's Department of War; where weapons suppliers are outfitting ICE to raid U.S. cities, with $205 million in DHS orders for guns, ammunition, and chemical munitions in a single month--but we can't make the barest gesture towards prevention beforehand, beyond preemptively teeing up our RUN, HIDE, FIGHT alerts. Where the White House's official Twitter account still, to this day, displays Trump's blatantly false announcement about a shooter in custody. But perhaps my most upsetting feeling that night--sheltering in place, manhunt underway--was tedium. This story has been seen, digested, and repeated with such regularity that it's now rote. The horrors of American gun violence are so banal that even the observation that they're banal is boring, and has been for years. For the families of the victims, today is a thunderbolt that might break them forever. For the students and others who spent hours in hiding, the trauma may be lifelong. But for everyone else, this was just the in America this year. Tomorrow, or the day after, there will be a new one to overshadow it, just as this tragedy overshadowed its . Sometimes the process is concurrent, like the at Evergreen High School that was immediately--simultaneously--eclipsed by the shooting of Charlie Kirk. It killed me, thinking about those kids in Colorado, seeing their governor fly their state's flags at for Kirk, rather than for the nightmare that had just consumed their community. And it kills me that none of the victims from Brown will get even the tiniest fraction of the mandatory mourning--the , the , the massive federal government that man, who their deaths were "worth it," were just the price you pay for freedom. Christina Paxson, the President of Brown University, said at one of the press briefings after the shooting, "This is a day one hopes never happens. And it has." At this late stage of our national death drive, that may be the only useful takeaway we can draw from these tragedies: Wherever we are, we hope it never happens in our city, our neighborhood, our school. And, eventually, it will. * ? P.S. That was a bit of a grim way to close. So, on a lighter note: Today was the first snow of the year. Here's a few pictures from sledding. | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Aaron Regunberg ? ?

body:
Next year, VoteVets will be twenty years old. That same year, our country will celebrate its 250th birthday.
#
Next year, VoteVets will be twenty years old.
That same year, our country will celebrate its 250th birthday.
To the former, it’s truly hard to believe that so much time has passed — even more difficult to believe how much we have accomplished together since then.
To the latter, I am sure it saddens you as much as it does me that we will celebrate this national milestone under the yoke of a man who seeks at every opportunity to destroy the very same democracy our ancestors founded all those years ago.
But I must tell you: I could not be prouder of how we have met that man and his authoritarian ambitions. VoteVets has fought this president to a standstill so many times, and we are poised to take back our majority in Congress next year. And it will be our candidates, VoteVets-backed leaders, who do it.
So, as we approach the final days of this year, I’m writing to make a humble request:
[**Would you please make a $5 donation so that we can finish 2025 out strong, and enter 2026 in fighting shape? There is much still to be done, and we can’t do it without your help.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-dec25.3jrL2s&refcode=em251214-1-160&t=1&akid=34818%2E3013755%2EvHoR9Q)

clean_body: Next year, VoteVets will be twenty years old. That same year, our country will celebrate its 250th birthday. Next year, VoteVets will be twenty years old. That same year, our country will celebrate its 250th birthday. To the former, it’s truly hard to believe that so much time has passed — even more difficult to believe how much we have accomplished together since then. To the latter, I am sure it saddens you as much as it does me that we will celebrate this national milestone under the yoke of a man who seeks at every opportunity to destroy the very same democracy our ancestors founded all those years ago. But I must tell you: I could not be prouder of how we have met that man and his authoritarian ambitions. VoteVets has fought this president to a standstill so many times, and we are poised to take back our majority in Congress next year. And it will be our candidates, VoteVets-backed leaders, who do it. So, as we approach the final days of this year, I’m writing to make a humble request: If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | ** | | | | | | | ** Thank you, Paul Eaton Major General, U.S. Army, Ret. clean_body: It’s one thing to TELL you that Emily is bringing your voices to the halls of Congress – it’s even better to SHOW you. | Peter, it’s one thing to TELL you that Emily is bringing your voices to the halls of Congress – it’s even better to SHOW you! Check out these two speeches: The Trump regime wants our immigrant and mixed-status neighbors to be afraid to fill out the Census so they will be undercounted, and Republicans can further tilt the political playing field in their favor. Insurance premiums are set to skyrocket – and instead of lowering costs, Republicans shut down the government. From her family’s experience, Emily understands that losing healthcare assistance can be devastating. And Emily isn’t just making speeches – she’s taking action. Thanks to your grassroots support, we’re building a robust political operation to help Democrats in competitive districts flip the House and deliver the change we need. Take a minute to watch and share these speeches – and if you want to see more leaders like Emily in Congress, chip in to fuel our organizing in 2026: If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = = Thank you 💙 – Team Emily | | Emily Randall is proud to represent Washington’s 6th District in Congress. She grew up in a working-class Union household in Port Orchard and was the first in her family to attend college. After serving in the State Senate and passing nearly 200 bills, Emily made history in 2024 as the first queer Latina elected to Congress. Now, Emily is fighting for WA working families in D.C. and organizing to take back the House. Can you chip in $6 or any amount and join our team? = Want to maximize your impact?Become a monthly donor to help us flip the House from red to blue! = Follow on: | | | | | | Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress Emily Randall for Congress P.O. name: Winning recipe
subject: ultra rare combination Democrats desperately need
body:
| | | | | [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001fG2tASl6Itm_EXdoek4spHGUtKzAUjc1AOTOrHoXb2pctOrTvN8wMrMVyKFS53KyGbluN58hciF6R-OxooqoBZnn0djdnO9AsO7f27uDxsH6qJphBI32UsRzYjBPDFUmhrd27ZLx372qt78qpx2ni96adlHHhsdLQ_69i5N3wUqQiD63LqY199kE1KWtVTihn7qrbh5hZDTAOSm-Mif3ncPeQaY5gBjC&c=ctMe5u9V1-76EAc--cWGBiWTSn-p_zfBYCPBbx_SfAER3SZ11MXN3Q==&ch=dn5CoX0BnTNML4nqgspGjjuSIPdUoPElggJbi-I4EkvE3NOGiGkTXw==)
---
|
Peter,
If 2025 has taught us anything, it's this: The Democrats are back.
Trump's approval is crashing. Even deep red districts are shifting blue by historic margins. The mandate is clear: voters want leaders who make life affordable, not chaotic.
Democrats have a rare opportunity right now: not just to take back the House from MAGA control, but to elect a new wave of Democratic leaders--a new generation ready to turn the page on the Trump era and begin to actually fix the issues facing our nation.
---
| | [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001fG2tASl6Itm_EXdoek4spHGUtKzAUjc1AOTOrHoXb2pctOrTvN8wMrMVyKFS53KyGbluN58hciF6R-OxooqoBZnn0djdnO9AsO7f27uDxsH6qJphBI32UsRzYjBPDFUmhrd27ZLx372qt78qpx2ni96adlHHhsdLQ_69i5N3wUqQiD63LqY199kE1KWtVTihn7qrbh5hZDTWW-IIQmZWchlvTVvbion4&c=ctMe5u9V1-76EAc--cWGBiWTSn-p_zfBYCPBbx_SfAER3SZ11MXN3Q==&ch=dn5CoX0BnTNML4nqgspGjjuSIPdUoPElggJbi-I4EkvE3NOGiGkTXw==)
---
|
It starts in Illinois' 9th District with Bushra Amiwala.
Bushra is running to succeed Democrat Jan Schakowsky in one of the bluest seats in the country. But she isn't just looking to fill a seat; she's building a movement. At age 27, Bushra brings a rare combination of youth and experience. She was the first woman of her generation to be elected to public office and has served on the school board for one of Chicago's largest school districts for 7 years.
---
| [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001fG2tASl6Itm_EXdoek4spHGUtKzAUjc1AOTOrHoXb2pctOrTvN8wMrMVyKFS53KyGbluN58hciF6R-OxooqoBZnn0djdnO9AsO7f27uDxsH6qJphBI32UsRzYjBPDFUmhrd27ZLx372qt78qpx2ni96adlHHhsdLQ_69i5N3wUqQiD63LqY199kE1KWtVTihn7qrbh5hZDTMYZhLm4GrgoYjst_baV6Q&c=ctMe5u9V1-76EAc--cWGBiWTSn-p_zfBYCPBbx_SfAER3SZ11MXN3Q==&ch=dn5CoX0BnTNML4nqgspGjjuSIPdUoPElggJbi-I4EkvE3NOGiGkTXw==)
---
|
Bushra knows politics as usual is failing our neighbors, which is why she is fighting for universal health care, tuition-free college, debt forgiveness, and affordable housing.
But because she refuses corporate and special interest money, she has a target on her back.
Our campaign is facing down MAGA billionaires and AIPAC who have jumped into this race to try and prevent Bushra from winning. They are terrified of a young, progressive woman they can't buy.
While they have deep pockets, our campaign has something more powerful: the people. [Please, can we count on you to chip in $25, $10, or whatever is meaningful to you to help elect Bushra and help Democrats seize on this rare opportunity?](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001fG2tASl6Itm_EXdoek4spHGUtKzAUjc1AOTOrHoXb2pctOrTvN8wMrMVyKFS53KynOMPVdwQDgYnrl_zWUJiSPd0UPJKKaTbgiNLGreVoV29tZGbLCwIh3fqdRMwROmKV7v-G0Wg8C5GMJDjDe7kUrlFluMT6kMGX9Pl5R1rUxGx3Rp_ufAbgLXn34Ud6pDfyj_anO7kLrWKO5LPhw3K5k6A8_Wi3WD4DnBxtES3wI4=&c=ctMe5u9V1-76EAc--cWGBiWTSn-p_zfBYCPBbx_SfAER3SZ11MXN3Q==&ch=dn5CoX0BnTNML4nqgspGjjuSIPdUoPElggJbi-I4EkvE3NOGiGkTXw==)

clean_body: | | | | | == | Peter, If 2025 has taught us anything, it's this: The Democrats are back. Trump's approval is crashing. Even deep red districts are shifting blue by historic margins. The mandate is clear: voters want leaders who make life affordable, not chaotic. Democrats have a rare opportunity right now: not just to take back the House from MAGA control, but to elect a new wave of Democratic leaders--a new generation ready to turn the page on the Trump era and begin to actually fix the issues facing our nation. | | == | It starts in Illinois' 9th District with Bushra Amiwala. Bushra is running to succeed Democrat Jan Schakowsky in one of the bluest seats in the country. But she isn't just looking to fill a seat; she's building a movement. At age 27, Bushra brings a rare combination of youth and experience. She was the first woman of her generation to be elected to public office and has served on the school board for one of Chicago's largest school districts for 7 years. | == | Bushra knows politics as usual is failing our neighbors, which is why she is fighting for universal health care, tuition-free college, debt forgiveness, and affordable housing. But because she refuses corporate and special interest money, she has a target on her back. Our campaign is facing down MAGA billionaires and AIPAC who have jumped into this race to try and prevent Bushra from winning. They are terrified of a young, progressive woman they can't buy. While they have deep pockets, our campaign has something more powerful: the people. == | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | == | Let's build the next generation of Democratic leadership. Team Bushra | | == | | | | == | Mail checks to: Bushra for Congress PO Box 911 Skokie, IL 60076 | | == | | | | Bushra for Congress | PO Box 911 | Skokie, IL 60076 US | |

clean_body: | | | Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth can’t walk this back The Washington Post reported that Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth gave a verbal order to kill everyone aboard a boat in the Caribbean – which would be a war crime. On Wednesday, Trump said he’s open to releasing the video footage of this second strike, but the administration is still going to cover its ass and act like they can get away with war crimes. We can not have a madman killing anyone he wants as the Defense Secretary. Help us keep the pressure building on Congress to do the right thing and IMPEACH Hegseth! Cedar Key Progress has decided to expand our billboard program to call for Hegseth’s impeachment – but we NEED your help. ** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. Thanks for your support, Cedar Key Progress | Cedar Key Progress support Democrats in competitive Southern districts who are building a diverse, inclusive, and just future. body:
# [](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1&akid=4018%2E582294%2EN9MN3Z)
Our biggest fundraising deadline yet is coming up on December 31st.
Here’s why we’re worried:
* New Hampshire's Senate race is going to be one of the most expensive and competitive races on the map in 2026.
* National Republicans and their right-wing billionaire donors are prepared to spend big to flip this seat.
* Polling has Chris Pappas and John Sununu in a statistical dead heat -- _**it’s nearly a tie.**_

We need a fundraising report that proves the strength of our campaign and enough money to execute all of our plans in 2026. That’s why it’s so important that we close out this year with a surge of grassroots support.
[**Will you chip in $5 or any amount today to show national Republicans that the Granite State is not for sale?**](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251214_1_worried.8Dr_YP&refcode=em20251214-1&t=2&akid=4018%2E582294%2EN9MN3Z)

Team Pappas

name: Team Moulton
subject: Accountability starts with us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Seth for Massachusetts Checks may also be mailed to:
body:
Leadership means holding yourself to the same standards you expect from others.

| |
---
Hi friend, Accountability shouldn’t be a slogan. It should be where leadership starts. Seth has always believed that trust in government begins with how we lead. That’s why his congressional office has been recognized four separate times by the nonpartisan Congressional Management Foundation for transparency, innovation, and public service. He’s called out complacency in both parties, pushed for age limits in Congress, and refused to treat leadership as a lifetime appointment. Because doing the right thing shouldn’t depend on who’s watching, it should be the expectation. As we reach the middle of December, this campaign is focused on showing that same kind of accountability in action. Every dollar we raise, every volunteer who steps up, every message we send — it all comes back to proving that Democrats can lead by example again. **[If you can, chip in by the 15th to help Seth keep building a campaign grounded in honesty, urgency, and results:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121273411/588916573/2090747700?refcode=251214_sfm_mm1_l1&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Y2UzMDc4OS0yNWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c9_Y9FdS2bCNxRJTgjVOdsZlaiejA03yeZlM9iYmTSA=)** | [Give $25 >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121273418/588916580/1375639745?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=251214_sfm_mm1&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Y2UzMDc4OS0yNWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c9_Y9FdS2bCNxRJTgjVOdsZlaiejA03yeZlM9iYmTSA=) | [Give $50 >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121273419/588916581/1375639745?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=251214_sfm_mm1&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Y2UzMDc4OS0yNWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c9_Y9FdS2bCNxRJTgjVOdsZlaiejA03yeZlM9iYmTSA=)

### About Seth Moulton

clean_body: Leadership means holding yourself to the same standards you expect from others. | | Hi friend, Accountability shouldn’t be a slogan. It should be where leadership starts. Seth has always believed that trust in government begins with how we lead. That’s why his congressional office has been recognized four separate times by the nonpartisan Congressional Management Foundation for transparency, innovation, and public service. He’s called out complacency in both parties, pushed for age limits in Congress, and refused to treat leadership as a lifetime appointment. Because doing the right thing shouldn’t depend on who’s watching, it should be the expectation. As we reach the middle of December, this campaign is focused on showing that same kind of accountability in action. Every dollar we raise, every volunteer who steps up, every message we send — it all comes back to proving that Democrats can lead by example again. = | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately. Thanks for being part of this team, Team Moulton | | | ---|---|--- About Seth Moulton Seth Moulton is a father, a husband, a Marine, and a Democrat with a mission to bring a new generation of leaders to Congress. He is the proud father of two girls, Emmy and Caroline, and husband to his wife, Liz. name: Team Cooper
subject: If Medicaid is gutted, then THIS happens
body:
Help elect a pro-Medicaid Senator.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2365?source=&t=1&akid=163%2E124072%2Ea8xerY)
Peter, if you want a Senator who will protect and expand access to Medicaid, Roy Cooper’s your guy — and Michael Whatley isn’t. **[Whatley admitted he’s running for Senate because Trump asked him to. He doesn’t care whether North Carolinians lose their Medicaid coverage — and he’s got MAGA Republican donors lining up to fund his campaign. Can you rush $2 right now to help us meet our mid-month goal?>>>](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2672?t=7&akid=163%2E124072%2Ea8xerY)** Michael Whatley supports the recent law that guts Medicaid and causes more than 690,000 North Carolinians to risk losing their health insurance. That includes low-income children and seniors in nursing homes. It puts rural hospitals at risk of closing. **_And it could increase premium costs by thousands of dollars._** **[Peter, these are the consequences if Roy doesn’t win this election: North Carolina won’t get a Senator dedicated to protecting Medicaid. We’ll miss a chance to flip the whole Senate blue — and we’ll lose the opportunity to stop the MAGA agenda in its tracks. So we have to ask: Can you give just $2 in the 2 days we have left before our mid-month fundraising deadline?](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2672?t=8&akid=163%2E124072%2Ea8xerY)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [DONATE $2](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2673?t=9&akid=163%2E124072%2Ea8xerY)

Thanks so much,
– Team Cooper
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=2&akid=163%2E124072%2Ea8xerY)**

name: Ben Waldon
subject: a tough call
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121274580/588920683/-2054079050?refcode=jtk1019-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDY0NmMzMC0yOGQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VuE67tgsT_e8LMkPkLQm6dKfSULM2CBCWctpHWo_L2U=)

Hi, team – it's Ben, Michael's campaign manager.
We had to make a tough call this week.
At our last Team Bennet meeting, it was clear we'd need a bigger budget heading into Q1 of the new year, even though we don't yet have the resources to cover it. **I gave the green light anyway.**
I know it sounds irresponsible. And it _is_ a risk. But as the election cycle ramps up in the new year, we have to kick this campaign into high gear. It'd be a mistake to not take risks now – and here's why:
With this budget, we can run ads about Michael's mission to rebuild Colorado's middle class and make this state's economy actually work for working families, not just the wealthy few. We can plan more campaign stops to reach voters from Fort Collins to Alamosa and everywhere in between. We can keep our digital outreach on the cutting edge.
Simply put, we can do what it takes to reach as many voters as we possibly can: on the air, online and in real life.
But we're not funded by corporate PACs. **Instead, we're betting that our base of grassroots supporters will come through by chipping in toward our $20,000 goal ahead of tomorrow night's mid-month deadline to help us make up the rest** – _if_ we have the courage to ask.
So with that said: **_[Will you rush a donation now to help us reach our mid-month goal and bridge our Q1 budget gap? Your investment of $5, $25 or any amount today will pay dividends when we win this race.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121274582/588920685/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1019-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDY0NmMzMC0yOGQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VuE67tgsT_e8LMkPkLQm6dKfSULM2CBCWctpHWo_L2U=)_**

name: Colin Allred
subject: I’m ending my race for U.S. Senate
body:
Peter,
I officially ended my campaign for U.S. Senate and filed to run for Congress in Texas’ 33rd Congressional District.
I didn’t come to this decision lightly – I’ve put everything I had into my Senate campaign over the last five months, traveling to every corner of the state, talking to as many Texans as I could, building on the incredible grassroots campaign we started last year.
While it was clear that many Texans were ready to support me to the end, it was also clear that the Democratic primary would end up in a bruising and costly runoff in May. And if we want to prevent another MAGA loyalist from representing our state for six more years, that’s simply a risk we can’t afford to take.
So, today I am announcing my campaign to represent the newly drawn 33rd district in Texas. If you’d still like to see me head to Washington in November, I hope you’ll consider splitting a donation between me and Real Texas to get my new campaign off to a strong start today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?refcode=121025_1]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Colin Allred and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?express_lane=true&refcode=121025_1&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?express_lane=true&refcode=121025_1&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?express_lane=true&refcode=121025_1&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?express_lane=true&refcode=121025_1&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?express_lane=true&refcode=121025_1&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?refcode=121025_1&amount=25]
District 33 was racially gerrymandered by Republicans in an effort to further rig our democracy, but it’s still the community where I grew up attending public schools and watching my mom struggle to pay for our groceries. It’s the community where Aly and I are raising our two boys. It is my home.
The threats that our democracy and our way of living are facing right now are real. It’s harder than ever for kids like me to get a shot, and for single moms like mine to afford their basic needs. But I am more confident than I have ever been that together, we can beat these threats. That’s why I am humbly asking for your help to return to Congress to fight for North Texans, our democracy, and the American Dream.
If you can, please split a donation of a few bucks between my campaign and Real Texas to help me get started. Thank you for reading, and for all of your continued support as we fight to get Texas representation we can be proud of. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?refcode=121025_1]
Colin Allred
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?refcode=121025_1 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?refcode=121025_1]

San Antonio, TX 78248

Only U.S. citizens or legal permanent residents are eligible to contribute. An Individual can contribute up to $5,000 to The Real Texas PAC per calendar year. Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. All contributions from corporations and labor unions will be deposited in the non-contribution account. Please contact us at realtexas@mbacg.com if you would like a different allocation.

All contributions from corporations and labor unions will be deposited in the non-contribution account. Please contact us at realtexas@mbacg.com if you would like a different allocation. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

name: Thomas, Team Katie Porter
subject: My job as finance director
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40569%2E3422442%2EN8DRtA)
Hey Peter,
I won’t lie, I’m getting worried. Our public fundraising deadline is coming up fast at the end of the year, and what we report shapes this race.
As Katie Porter’s finance director, I’m working hard to make sure we have the resources to win. That means raising more money and making smart investments to keep our fundraising on track with our strategy to win.
Part of my job is making sure we hit our big goals, and part of making sure we’re on pace is by breaking big tasks into smaller ones. **[That’s why we set a mid-month goal of raising $10,000 over email by midnight on the 15th. Can you chip in today to help us reach it?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251214_1_ak.4XF2i4&refcode=em20251214-40569&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=40569%2E3422442%2EN8DRtA)**
I know our grassroots donors have the power to reach this goal (I see the numbers—even if a small percent of people chip in we’ll hit it with ease). That’s why I’m writing to you today.
**[I hope I’ve explained the situation we’re in, and why your donation matters so much right now. With all that said: can I count on you to donate $5 or more to Katie Porter’s campaign today?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251214_1_ak.4XF2i4&refcode=em20251214-40569&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=40569%2E3422442%2EN8DRtA)**
[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251214_1_ak.4XF2i4&refcode=em20251214-40569&t=23&akid=40569%2E3422442%2EN8DRtA)
Thank you,
Thomas,
Finance Director
Katie Porter for Governor

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20251214-40569&amounts=&t=24&refcode2=40569_3422442_N8DRtA&akid=40569%2E3422442%2EN8DRtA)**
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4g-emails?source=40569&t=25&akid=40569%2E3422442%2EN8DRtA)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4g-em-update-info-footer?source=40569)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4g-unsubscribe?source=40569&t=26&akid=40569%2E3422442%2EN8DRtA)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Governor 2026
PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)

body:
It's the holiday season, but I'm keeping an eye on our books to make sure we're ready for 2026. Can you help before Monday’s deadline? |

Hi there! Monica here from the AZ Dems finance team. (Sorry about the silly subject line, I promise I’m very excited about the holidays!) While for most people this time of year means buying last-minute presents and getting ready to travel, for me it also means keeping an eye on our books to make sure we have all the resources we need heading into 2026. Our mid-month deadline is Monday, and I wanted to remind you what's at stake — and what we're doing to prepare. And if you’d rather skip ahead and support us now, you can do so here. ❗**The stakes:** We're less than a year out from elections that will decide control of Congress and determine whether Katie Hobbs, Kris Mayes, Adrian Fontes, and other Democratic leaders can keep delivering for Arizona. Katie is statistically tied with her Republican challengers. The path to flipping the House runs through Arizona. And political analysts are still writing us off, pointing to the doubled Republican registration advantage. ✅**What we're doing:** We're working to organize year-round in all 15 counties. We're registering new voters. We're defending Arizona Democrats who are leading the national resistance to Trump — from Mark Kelly standing up to DoD and FBI intimidation, to Kris Mayes challenging his illegal tariffs, to Ruben Gallego fighting for working families facing inflation and healthcare chaos. And we're holding Arizona Republicans accountable when they lie about healthcare, call Democrats "terrorists," attack federal judges, etc. [**So, to help continue this work, can I count on you to support our work with $10, $25, or whatever you can before Monday’s mid-month deadline?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121274804/588921314/79744751?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_MMG_AC_20251214_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODQ1OWY0YmUtMjhkOS1mMDExLTgxOTYtMDAwZDNhMWQ1MjE1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=f8WC9RS70EDysH2hhRFKeuHMt8Mf95H2RLPPByvoZBE=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121274806/588921315/79744751?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_MMG_AC_20251214_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODQ1OWY0YmUtMjhkOS1mMDExLTgxOTYtMDAwZDNhMWQ1MjE1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=f8WC9RS70EDysH2hhRFKeuHMt8Mf95H2RLPPByvoZBE=)

clean_body: It's the holiday season, but I'm keeping an eye on our books to make sure we're ready for 2026. Can you help before Monday’s deadline? | = Hi there! Monica here from the AZ Dems finance team. (Sorry about the silly subject line, I promise I’m very excited about the holidays!) While for most people this time of year means buying last-minute presents and getting ready to travel, for me it also means keeping an eye on our books to make sure we have all the resources we need heading into 2026. Our mid-month deadline is Monday, and I wanted to remind you what's at stake — and what we're doing to prepare. And if you’d rather skip ahead and support us now, you can do so here. ❗The stakes: We're less than a year out from elections that will decide control of Congress and determine whether Katie Hobbs, Kris Mayes, Adrian Fontes, and other Democratic leaders can keep delivering for Arizona. Katie is statistically tied with her Republican challengers. The path to flipping the House runs through Arizona. And political analysts are still writing us off, pointing to the doubled Republican registration advantage. ✅What we're doing: We're working to organize year-round in all 15 counties. We're registering new voters. We're defending Arizona Democrats who are leading the national resistance to Trump — from Mark Kelly standing up to DoD and FBI intimidation, to Kris Mayes challenging his illegal tariffs, to Ruben Gallego fighting for working families facing inflation and healthcare chaos. And we're holding Arizona Republicans accountable when they lie about healthcare, call Democrats "terrorists," attack federal judges, etc. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = I know it's a busy time. But the work we're doing right now — building organizing capacity, registering voters, supporting candidates who will stand up to MAGA extremism — that's what wins close races in Arizona. Thanks for everything you do, Monica Tang Finance team, Arizona Democratic Party = Arizona Democratic Party PO Box 36123 Phoenix, AZ 85067-6123 United States mailto:info@azdem.org | PAID FOR BY THE ARIZONA DEMOCRATIC PARTY. disclaimer_text: Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121274896/588921668/-272948131?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_MMG_AC_20251214_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxZmFlNzk0Zi0yYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YhoMvmglmsEEArQRaEuz9Wyy6hajD3xsSV2G90B8Bsc=&emci=7120ce48-2ad9-f011-8196-000d3a1d5215&emdi=1fae794f-2ad9-f011-8196-000d3a1d5215&ceid=252045)
---

clean_body: The fight for liberty and freedom has NEVER been as important as it is at this very moment. | | | | Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this Peter, The fight for liberty and freedom has NEVER been as important as it is at this very moment. Here 's why. 💰 OUR NATIONAL DEBT Right now, our national debt sits at over $38 TRILLION. That equates to more than $266,000 per taxpayer - and that number is growing every single day. Instead of buckling down, doing the tough work, and reining in wasteful spending, Congress passes spending bill after spending bill, as if money grows on trees. But the worst part of this entire saga? We 're borrowing money from our greatest adversary, China. Peter, that leads me to my next point... 🪖 NEVER-ENDING WARS Members of Congress are so focused on being the world's police and fixing problems in every other country, that they Think about it, Peter. We're borrowing money from China to send to Ukraine, Israel, and every other country that believes we're nothing more than an ATM. Any time a conflict breaks out, we're expected to get involved, pick winners and losers, and fund both sides of a war we have no vested interest in. And it doesn't matter how much it costs - either monetarily or with the lives of our Armed Forces - Washington elites willingly send our sons and daughters into harm's way. Here 's the God's honest truth, Peter. Washington elites make these decisions because it 's NOT their sons or daughters being sent into a war zone. They're NOT on the receiving end of a devastating phone call or a visit from a Casualty Notification Officer who informs them a child was killed in action thousands of miles away for an unknown cause. Peter, these elitists spend YOUR hard-earned tax dollars willy-nilly because it's NOT their checkbooks they have to balance. After all, they aren 't going to lose their homes or cars if America's checkbook is in the red. And that leads me to my final point ... 🔔 PROTECTING LIBERTY With all of their crazy spending, forever wars, and constant attacks on the Constitution, Washington elites chip away at liberty, little by little. They collude with Big Tech to censor your thoughts and opinions... They gatekeep and decide what's fact versus fiction... They shove Big Government down our throats as the solution to any and every problem. With control of all three branches of government, Republicans have an opportunity to show Americans we can - and will - make life better for the American people. We can show Americans we 'll uphold our Constitutional rights and defend liberty at ALL costs. In liberty, Rand Paul | | | | | | Paid for by Rand Paul for Senate | | P.O. name: Lily Tang Williams
subject: THE RACE FOR NH02 IS ALMOST TIED -- MERRY CHRISTMAS!
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/lilytangwilliams/the-race-for-nh02-is-almost-tied-merry-christmas?e=c338950e03)
---
| |
---
| |
# THE RACE FOR NH02 IS ALMOST TIED
# MERRY CHRISTMAS!

**Recent University Poll on NH02 **

According to recent polling, my race to flip New Hampshire’s 2nd Congressional District is almost TIED. Together, we really can flip this important seat in the U.S. House of Representatives!
Let me remind you of a few of the many ways Maggie Goodlander has already taken a wrecking ball to our Republic and our democratic process:

• Most recently, AFTER the poll, Goodlander was featured in the “Seditious Six” video that encouraged our military to disobey so-called unlawful orders from President Trump. The implications of her video are terrifying. [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7xKNYWgOPKc](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=ff66fe6ced&e=c338950e03) • She supported her party to keep our government shut down, leaving those same military men and women without pay.

• Goodlander co-authored the House Judiciary Committee’s report that led to the 2019 impeachment of President Donald Trump.

• She served as a Deputy Attorney General in Merrick Garland’s corrupt Department of Justice which trampled over our Constitution by targeting Joe Biden’s political opponents.

My opponent has ties to deep-pocket donors like the George Soros family and other deep state elites. Her husband, Jake Sullivan, and Hillary Clinton are behind her campaign. On short order, they could fill her war chest with dark money in an attempt to diminish our momentum.

However -- together, this is a race we can WIN, and it could be the race to save the House Republican Majority. That’s why I’m calling on you to chip in whatever you can before my end-of-year deadline.

[ [RUSH $20 TO LILY TANG WILLIAMS](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=e311e313ef&e=c338950e03) ]

**My First Christmas in America**

After my first encounter with Santa in 1982 on my college campus in Shanghai,
(me at the bottom looking back)
I celebrated my first real Christmas after arriving in Austin, TX, in 1988.

John, my future husband, and I were dating in graduate school. His parents invited me to join their family for Christmas, and for the first time I received Christmas gifts: a stocking and a huge Texas-sized lodge shirt from John’s mom.

We had dinner together, listened to Christmas songs, and watched the movie “It’s a Wonderful Life.” John’s entire family made me feel at home, and I learned a little bit more about Christmas as a holiday and appreciated the spirit of giving.

Over the years, I have learned more about our country’s founding, culture, concept of individual rights, liberty, and freedom of religion. I found God in America.

I’m answering my calling to run for Congress as a proud US citizen and patriot. I want to serve the country that gave me a life, a family, an opportunity to succeed, and the precious freedom we all share.

Merry Christmas! I’d love your support in my fight to save the American Dream. Thank you!

[ [DONATE TO LILY TODAY](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=08fe668bf9&e=c338950e03)]

God Bless America!

name: NDRC
subject: Republicans are pouring millions into Missouri
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
Missouri is putting on full display the lengths to which map manipulators are willing to go to silence voters.
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121275716/588924385/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjNhMDAzN2RlLTJmZDktZjAxMS04MTk2LTAwMGQzYTFkNTIxNSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=7fs4jggKVn8vpPR1-dAOJW6CBqWLztaMHqh3tm_hGUk=&emci=633674bc-b8d6-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=3a0037de-2fd9-f011-8196-000d3a1d5215&ceid=8431045)
---

**First:** Missouri Republicans rammed through a gerrymandered map with the intention of handing Donald Trump an additional Republican seat in the House. **Then:** Missouri voters fought back immediately, gathering signatures for a ballot initiative rejecting the gerrymandered map. **Now:** Extreme map manipulators — including people in Trump’s inner circle — are running scared that Missouri voters will reject this map, and so they’re pouring millions of dollars into a campaign to stop them. Peter, we’ll put it plainly: This is a fight we simply have to win. If Missouri voters reject this map, it will send a resounding message that voters reject the extreme, partisan gerrymandering we’re seeing sweep across this country. **But Trump and his big money interests aren’t going to let up this fight — and so we need the resources to fight back.[Will you pitch in to fund the NDRC’s work fighting back in Missouri and every state attempting to rig the next election?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121275720/588924391/-1027206931?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_Republicans2MilMO_email_20251214-NDRC-Tier1-Republicans2MilMO_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251214-NDRC-Tier1-Republicans2MilMO_&utm_campaign=Republicans2MilMO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjNhMDAzN2RlLTJmZDktZjAxMS04MTk2LTAwMGQzYTFkNTIxNSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=7fs4jggKVn8vpPR1-dAOJW6CBqWLztaMHqh3tm_hGUk=)** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

Now we can see how far Republicans are willing to go to silence the will of the people. Lawmakers forced through this gerrymander despite massive outrage from Missouri voters, who flooded Governor Mike Kehoe’s office with calls opposing the gerrymander and protested across the state.
And now, powerful, Trump-aligned interests are sparing no expense to defeat a voter-led effort to overturn this gerrymander.
But here’s the good news: This infusion of money shows that they know this gerrymander is deeply unpopular. And if Missourians vote it down, it would be a massive victory for the fair maps movement — not to mention, send a message to other states seeking to rig maps for the president.

clean_body: Missouri is putting on full display the lengths to which map manipulators are willing to go to silence voters. | First: Missouri Republicans rammed through a gerrymandered map with the intention of handing Donald Trump an additional Republican seat in the House. Then: Missouri voters fought back immediately, gathering signatures for a ballot initiative rejecting the gerrymandered map. Now: Extreme map manipulators — including people in Trump’s inner circle — are running scared that Missouri voters will reject this map, and so they’re pouring millions of dollars into a campaign to stop them. Peter, we’ll put it plainly: This is a fight we simply have to win. If Missouri voters reject this map, it will send a resounding message that voters reject the extreme, partisan gerrymandering we’re seeing sweep across this country. But Trump and his big money interests aren’t going to let up this fight — and so we need the resources to fight back.= | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Now we can see how far Republicans are willing to go to silence the will of the people. But here’s the good news: This infusion of money shows that they know this gerrymander is deeply unpopular. | | Hi, it’s Oregon Secretary of State, Tobias Read. **The Trump Administration recently announced that it will withhold SNAP food assistance from families in Oregon and other Democratic-led states — unless we hand over private data about our residents, including names and immigration status.**
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121276011/588925385/-430637131?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NDE5MzRlOS0zM2Q5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8afgSBYx3n69BD58RPRJPGDVsiSZHntNyDQX4Z2GUdw=&emci=55a2f4e4-09d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=941934e9-33d9-f011-8196-000d3a1d5215&ceid=30662126)

Let me be absolutely clear: This is political blackmail. It’s unlawful. And it will take food off the tables of kids, seniors, and working families. **[That’s why I’m launching an emergency petition, and I need your immediate response. Do you oppose President Trump’s plan to block food assistance for Oregon families? Add your name now.>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121276012/588925386/-430637131?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NDE5MzRlOS0zM2Q5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8afgSBYx3n69BD58RPRJPGDVsiSZHntNyDQX4Z2GUdw=&emci=55a2f4e4-09d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=941934e9-33d9-f011-8196-000d3a1d5215&ceid=30662126)**

Oregon has already said **NO** to President Trump’s unlawful demand — _loudly and clearly._ Oregon will not be complicit in taking food away from kids and families so a billionaire politician and his lackeys in D.C. can take revenge on their opponents. But here’s the truth: Donald Trump will keep pushing, threatening, and trying to punish states like ours for daring to stand up to him. That’s why we’ll need a strong grassroots foundation to keep up the fight in the months ahead. **So right now, I’m gathering as many signatures as possible to show that Oregonians — and Americans everywhere — refuse to let any president weaponize hunger for political gain.** [**Will you add your name to say loud and clear: NO, Trump cannot take food away from Oregon families?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121276014/588925388/-430637131?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NDE5MzRlOS0zM2Q5LWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDUyMTUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8afgSBYx3n69BD58RPRJPGDVsiSZHntNyDQX4Z2GUdw=&emci=55a2f4e4-09d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=941934e9-33d9-f011-8196-000d3a1d5215&ceid=30662126) Thank you,Tobias Read

subject: Something bigger is broken.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARK LAMB FOR CONGRESS
body:

Fellow Patriot,

Long before I ever put on a badge, I made a simple promise to myself:

When something is broken, you don't complain - _you fix it._

That philosophy guided me through eight years as Sheriff. It's why I stood up to cartels, why I defended our border communities, and why I backed our deputies even when it wasn't politically convenient.

But today, something bigger is broken.

Washington has stopped listening to the people who make this country work. Not the lobbyists. Not the bureaucrats. _The people_.

The ranchers who wake up before dawn. The small business owners who sign both sides of the paycheck. The parents who are fighting to protect their kids' future. The families who still believe in God, Family, and Freedom.

They deserve a voice that won't fold when things get tough.

**That's why I'm running for Congress - not to join the political class, but to challenge it.** To restore the kind of leadership that answers to the people, not to the unelected and unaccountable. And I'm going to fight for that every single day. But I can't do it alone.

If you believe America needs leaders who show up, tell the truth, and stand firm - **[I'm asking you to join this mission.](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=ca9f2e972dfa49d3a3c30bd0f92a12c0&_e=2odvXciXqpfy4LP4iJrvFCPCZYqbqrmEzDxl_TLIKZS5CJr_NtD_YL1ZFmMfcV4e6cGY6QBhDkbQpJrh3soCS18A6NZ9__yt_TMRNNWAc0IkP4AbBjkKWIjt07NFYc2tRoDgE7SGtV4ZnPZ7mDZ1ZjR5qHE8-lHIbNMe0A37lLgHMOOb6Qxn2ghw4Od4iYU7BiIHSY2US6fx3BQ0oevjawqIv06t65XBKmexP_paS5p5jCwkCoksWJGtEoSOV8zPEGgwNXHjYTBXuoUWrn6YBLx_I-2oMl7EBJqjP-aDw-R0X1Kkv_SyyRFNWEvF3bEuYqNtG-NH7Q08sBrjxWIjOYDDGNurOyfzYzESFeCXubLIOXiyg_uFVQF7CoQttcmLkgzQSqWXLPr-U2Fd9QbmoV4Vg_lj8n8lNN9HO4uqwzZjLpckRYQhY_AEUhq5EiTFhfoYkEzhoavs83qh8ARTbwzachs3R9qsO8Us6BnF8Cj0eO-nTxrLwQturNCUAnieK5O2AJ7Rs0qEWyOiJ43ZI0h28fGNp6pHFTpeIe2WkX_l-vKU2O7YV72YxnBAJgrUO4HvU2xGFuS5-82n_wFYSb8wxK66zV6r3IoKOrTpaQk5MMKInPXjq3sxEWreTTedxVGevsWxDeIMESQWIxCi3zAVc1_cKZArXOlqDA9TuNU%3D)**

clean_body: When something is broken, you don't complain — you fix it. | Fellow Patriot, Long before I ever put on a badge, I made a simple promise to myself: When something is broken, you don't complain - you fix it. That philosophy guided me through eight years as Sheriff. It's why I stood up to cartels, why I defended our border communities, and why I backed our deputies even when it wasn't politically convenient. But today, something bigger is broken. Washington has stopped listening to the people who make this country work. Not the lobbyists. Not the bureaucrats. The people. The ranchers who wake up before dawn. The small business owners who sign both sides of the paycheck. The parents who are fighting to protect their kids' future. The families who still believe in God, Family, and Freedom. They deserve a voice that won't fold when things get tough. That's why I'm running for Congress - not to join the political class, but to challenge it. To restore the kind of leadership that answers to the people, not to the unelected and unaccountable. And I'm going to fight for that every single day. But I can't do it alone. If you believe America needs leaders who show up, tell the truth, and stand firm - Thank you for being in this fight, Mark Lamb Republican for Congress, AZ-5 Join Mark Lamb on social media! | | PAID FOR BY MARK LAMB FOR CONGRESS Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go: .

_**by Cliff Schecter**_
In just 17 days, we perform all the usual rituals. An oversized disco ball drops in Times Square. We raise glasses of bubbly and toast to the future. And we share resolutions most will never keep.
And this year we have a special addition--special in the sense that Patrick Bateman (Christian Bale) was special in _American Psycho_. Because when the clock strikes midnight on December 31st, 2025, Republicans will rob 22 million Americans of their ability to access treatment should they fall ill or become injured.
They will do this knowing full well their callous indifference will cause untold suffering _and kill Americans --including millions of Republicans. _Marking the end of the GOP as any kind of long-term party in America.
Yes, folks, this group of selfish, greedy, sleazy, pedophile-protecting clods--who should be charged with trademark infringement for using the same name and symbol for their feudal, multi-level marketing scheme as American heroes like Lincoln, Grant, TR, and Ike used to run for office--become more preposterous by the day.
* * *
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.BUKkq12uG1rLJ2BjbNyxWVZquLnorwOXNl95y08mZR0?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=181557483)
* * *
[Like these parasites who rely on Trump to tell them what to think, say, and when to go potty, also sink further into a dementia mirror-universe because he does](https://substack.com/redirect/65abcec7-fc35-430b-b498-7bb8fc743eb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
There are certainly no lack of examples of their glib dishonesty and putrid amorality. But if you want one that perfectly sums up the Jonestown GOP Caucus, you couldn't do much better than their nonchalance at allowing enhanced Obamacare subsidies, the only way millions of Americans can afford healthcare, to just disappear.
Like Mike Johnson's testicles when Trump enters the room.
Ok, so here's your basic story, in all it's repulsive Republican perfidy:
On December 11th, the U.S. Senate held two votes, rejecting a Democratic attempt to address the imminent expiration of _Affordable Care Act_ (Obamacare) premium subsidies _that 've helped cap costs for millions of Americans_.
The Democratic bill would've extended these life-saving subsidies for three years.
But Republicans rejected it, because they ~~dutifully care about fixing the health care system~~ are owned by the sick, elite predators who possess Epstein ethics. So they put Senate Leader John Thune out front. A man only there to add a bit of charm to their chronic manipulation, cruel deceit, and decrepit superego.
Basically, Thune is the GOP version of drawing a smiley face on a turd and then claiming it smells like roses.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f0549611-2e53-479e-b6b2-f1ed81ac2951?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
So Thune filibustered this simple, common-sense bill to extend subsidies, and it failed to reach the 60 votes needed under the (anti-constitutional) filibuster. Then he offered an, um... "_alternative_." I've been advised by legal counsel that I can't call it a _plan,_ [or I'd open myself up to lawsuits from libeled, pissed-off real plans](https://substack.com/redirect/8705b7c4-5c57-48ef-8f90-cbc76576c323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
This jerk-store offering _would terminate all additional ACA subsidies,_ diverting--and I'm not joking--**$1,000 -$1,500 per person **to health savings accounts (HSAs). Why didn't the GOP just go for broke and offer a few scratch 'n sniff lottery tickets, New York City Subway tokens circa 1999 and a few used tissues they found in their pockets?
To state the obvious, [$1,000 ](https://substack.com/redirect/4052b330-c043-49b8-8d2a-285aebbf1a55?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[is](https://substack.com/redirect/4052b330-c043-49b8-8d2a-285aebbf1a55?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/4052b330-c043-49b8-8d2a-285aebbf1a55?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[far below what anyone needs to afford coverage](https://substack.com/redirect/4052b330-c043-49b8-8d2a-285aebbf1a55?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). _It's just an enormous scam, like Karoline Leavitt saying she married for love as she retrieves her husband's bedpan.
So, **Health care premiums will skyrocket for up to 24 million Americans,**_**one in eleven of us**_**,** on January 1st, but, hey, they'll have that tax-free $1000! Don't spend it all in one place!
Ok, actually, they won't have that, because it was smartly voted down for being so stupid it failed Trump's cognitive test. If I recall, it said: "man, person, woman, Trump recession, you pardoned a huge Honduran drug trafficker, so stop with your horseshit lies about Venezuela."
Close, but no porn star during Shark Week, if you will.
* * *
Subscribe for free today!
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.BUKkq12uG1rLJ2BjbNyxWVZquLnorwOXNl95y08mZR0?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=181557483)
* * *
Meanwhile, everyone with an ounce of intellectual honesty--in other words, [almost nobody in today's corporate media](https://substack.com/redirect/65abcec7-fc35-430b-b498-7bb8fc743eb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--will offer the context that this party of amalgamated chum has claimed for a decade they have a "plan." Just to fart out a term paper an hour before class.
One that's g _rossly insufficient and a middle finger_ to every single person purchasing insurance on ACA exchanges. Just a reminder, the ACA subsidies enacted during the COVID era _substantially reduced premiums_ and expanded coverage; letting them lapse means replacing a working system with the intellectual equivalent of supply-side economics or creation science. ****
**And Americans --including many Republicans, which we'll get to in a minute--will be incredibly pissed and inevitably die** because of it. Meanwhile, Thune and Fox & Friends will jet off on their Christmas vacations, not a care in the world. I mean, what would they worry about? We pay for their very good, guaranteed healthcare!
But don't worry, these shape-shifting, DOGE-infected imps offering a whole $1,000 "tax-advantaged" credit****_ensured the top marginal tax rate was lowered from 39% to 37.6%_**.** So malfunctioning protocol droid cum portly yacht-kateer raised by Apartheid AI--also known as Elon Musk--will be just fine as he gets the first trillion-dollar payoff in world history (while sucking at his job).
Also somehow getting by, the $40 million second-wedding man, and the slimy, sweaty guy who won't STFU about his puerile, Anti-Christ theory while in a totally non-concerning way is collecting data on _all of us_.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f0549611-2e53-479e-b6b2-f1ed81ac2951?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
That should make us all feel better, we and the people we know suffer directly because of their abject cruelty. I mean, hey, you may have a preventable fracture or 103.5 fever, but at least Republicans have engaged in an orgiastic showering of tax breaks and subsidies on the billionaire objects of their eternal affection.
The only other thing they can do that provides that dopamine-ketamine hit like they're visiting their unofficial party headquarters on Epstein Island.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ce7941e5-6e47-4f93-87ec-8b6b54ded24a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
You want a dopamine hit of your own, though? Ever see a modern political party purposely self-immolate? You haven't?? Weren't around for The Whigs? Well, then you have quite a treat coming your way.
Trump is already as popular as untreated chlamydia, and increasingly, the bankrupt farmers, fired government workers, and failed small businesses because of someone's stupid tariffs are leading his voters to turn against him. Not to mention the Epstein cover-up.
So this jack-wad with the 36% approval rating--assisted by his little Senate helpers--[is now going to kick many of these 24 million Americans off Obamacare due to nothing but cruelty](https://substack.com/redirect/9b3ac3c1-ffdc-42f4-bc7b-6b4a8ae7ff53?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--and surprise(!), half of them are Republicans.
Of those, 6 in 10 support extending the subsidies, as do 8 in 10 independents.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9b3ac3c1-ffdc-42f4-bc7b-6b4a8ae7ff53?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
So ya know, good luck with things when you add that five or 10, perhaps 15 million people to the record number who already realize they were conned by a diaper-clad doofus who can't stay awake for 20 minutes during a meeting.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c7f059b9-eaa6-43a8-a7c0-05bcde01e997?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
After the recent election destruction, with Republicans losing 14-16% of their support, it is realistic to ask if the GOP will just be a regional rump party in 15-20 mostly rural states with the GDP of a block in New York City. Maybe 11th Avenue in the 70s.
The level of utter political idiocy Republicans are about to engage in is hard to overestimate. But that's what having ideologues, cranks, fundies, freaks, and Scott Jennings as your base will do.
So, in my best _Marco from Tropoja_ voice from the film, "Taken": "Good Luck."
* * *
